He is also an idealist who has a naive infatuation with the
Delaz Fleet leader Aiguille Delaz.
During the Delaz Conflict, he puts his life on the line to
realize the dream that Operation Stardust represents.

A Delaz Fleet pilot.
During the One Year War, he was a Zeon soldier assigned to the 302nd Patrol Company commanded by Anavel Gato. 
During the Delaz Conflict he served as Anavel Gato's trusty right hand man.
Piloting a Rick Dom II, he acted as Gato's guard.
At the end of the Delaz Conflict, however, Anavel Gato entrusted him with the protection of Nina Purpleton, and he left the battleground for the safety of the Axis Advance Fleet's battleship Gwanzan.

A Zeon remnant pilot who attacks the Torrington base along
with Anavel Gato. He is a skilled veteran pilot who survived
the One Year War.
During the operation to steal the Gundam GP02, he helps
Anavel Gato escape by heading into the Federation base with
just three machines to fight against its MS forces.

A Zeon remnant pilot who attacks the Torrington base along
with Anavel Gato. He is a skilled veteran pilot who survived
the One Year War.
During the operation to steal the Gundam GP02, he deals
heavy damage to the Torrington base with long-ranged attacks
from his heavy MS Xamel.
After escaping the Torrington base, he coordinates with
Anavel Gato and retreats along with the Gundam GP02,
fulfilling his role by attacking the pursuing enemy troops.
Bob had the highest rank among the three who attacked the
Torrington base, and his helmet was decorated with a tusk.

Commander of the Cima Fleet, which was part of the Delaz Fleet. 
During the One Year War, she served as commander of the Armored Assault Force's Cima Marine Amphibious Unit.
Though she commands a fleet, she still fights on the front lines aboard her MS.
She looks for advantages rather than principles, and does not think twice about betraying people. But she isn't entirely selfish and is still popular among her subordinates.
She has high skills as a pilot, and is also an excellent commander who has molded her wayward group into a highly powerful fleet. 
During the One Year War she used her status in the pirate trade to make her own connections and hone her skills as a schemer.
She does not hesitate to betray others in return for her own survival, and her tenuous grasp on life can be compared to that of a mayfly.

Aide to the Cima Fleet, which was part of the Delaz Fleet.
He is in charge of protecting the fleet flagship Lili
Marleen when his commander Cima Garahau is fighting on the
front lines.
He has been Cima Garahau's right hand man since the One Year
War, and despite his skills he looks more like a ruffian
than a soldier.
When Cima Garahau rebelled during the Delaz Conflict, he
followed her orders and occupied the bridge of the Delaz
Fleet's flagship Gwaden.

A one-armed junk dealer living in the lunar city of Von Braun.
He fought alongside Anavel Gato, the "Nightmare of Solomon," during the One Year War. They had mutual respect for each other, but after he lost his left arm during battle he was forced to leave his position as a pilot.
He has a modest personality and does not speak much, but he is a loyal man who had his heart set on being a pilot. After the One Year War he operated a junk shop with the intention of one day resuming his role as a pilot.
For this purpose he continued to fix the discarded mobile armor Val-Walo. When he meets the Federation Forces pilot Kou Uraki, he decides to try his luck once again.

Commander of the Kimberlide base, established in a former
diamond mine. He is a talented commander with great
achievements as part of the Earth Occupation Force's African
forces during the One Year War.
His high leadership skills and personality won him the trust
of his comrades, and during the Delaz Conflict he donated an
HLV to Anavel Gato so that the stolen Gundam GP02 could be
taken to space. He also led his MS forces to delay the
pursuing Federation Forces. He was called a "model soldier"
by Gato, and lived up to this title.

The captain of the Earth Federation Forces' counter-sabotage special investigation brigade BGST ("Barghest"), also known as the Chivalry Squad.
He is the eldest son of the Tsurugi clan, the family that founded BGST, and he succeeded his father, Kanesada Tsurugi, as commander in U.C. 0084.
Holding the rank of lieutenant colonel, he is affectionately called Youngling by team members.
The highly skilled pilot of the Striker Custom, a unique model specializing in melee combat, he possesses keen insight and can identify those who have colluded with Zeon remnants even with limited information.
After the One Year War, he found himself troubled by the growing economic disparity within the Earth Sphere.
Determined to root out the corruption in the Federation government, he confronts not only Zeon forces, but also various EFF factions, from the Titans to the True Federal.

A member of the Halloween Squad aboard the AEUG ship Zwickbau.
She is highly regarded for her Newtype abilities by those around her.

A naive and sensitive boy from Side 7's Green Noa 1 colony, Kamille grew up throwing himself into karate and junior mobile suit sports to rebel against his feminine name and neglectful parents.
After a run-in with the Federation's elite Titans special forces, he gets caught up in an AEUG raid, and ends up helping steal the Gundam Mk-II. The teen has been a part of the AEUG ever since, as the invaluable pilot of the Gundam Mk-II.
Initially, Kamille only fought because he fell into situations that called for it, but after many meetings and partings, he started to find his own path and developed into a central figure in the AEUG.
At the same time, his innate Newtype powers began to develop. By awakening as a Newtype, he sees the potential for humanity to communicate without misunderstandings, and he decides to fight to eliminate those who would let their own shortsightedness prevent that from happening.

An ace pilot of the AEUG and a central figure supporting the organization, who is greatly trusted by others.
He is always calm and has the ability to anticipate the true qualities of others. He is also a charismatic leader and is strict with Kamille Bidan, but sees the boy's potential and tries to use his own experience and ideals to guide him down the right path.
He is a veteran mobile suit pilot who is skilled on the battlefield, capable of overwhelming opponents.
In addition to leading MS teams, he also uses his accurate judgment and leadership skills to strategize, negotiate, and act as a bodyguard, making him a central and well-rounded figure within the AEUG.
During the Gryps Conflict he threw himself into fighting against the Titans, wanting to make right the wrongdoings of the corrupt Earth Federation.

An ace pilot of the AEUG and a central figure supporting the organization, who is greatly trusted by others.
He is always calm and has the ability to anticipate the true qualities of others. He is also a charismatic leader and is strict with Kamille Bidan, but sees the boy's potential and tries to use his own experience and ideals to guide him down the right path.
He is a veteran mobile suit pilot who is skilled on the battlefield, capable of overwhelming opponents.
In addition to leading MS teams, he also uses his accurate judgment and leadership skills to strategize, negotiate, and act as a bodyguard, making him a central and well-rounded figure within the AEUG.
During the Gryps Conflict he threw himself into fighting against the Titans, wanting to make right the wrongdoings of the corrupt Earth Federation.

A subordinate of AEUG pilot Quattro Bajeena. He is a veteran soldier who survived the One Year War.
He has a happy and talkative personality, and is the jokester of the group. 
He is also a caring person who acts as a big brother figure to junior AEUG pilots like Kamille Bidan and Fa Yuiry.

A subordinate of AEUG pilot Quattro Bajeena. He is a veteran soldier who survived the One Year War.
He has a vigorous personality and enjoys fighting on the front lines, and although he sometimes uses outrageous battle tactics, he is skilled and trusted by his comrades.
He may look fierce, but in reality he is very understanding and popular within the AEUG.

A member of the AEUG. She has an exceptionally good memory and works in intelligence.
She is a curious thrill-seeker, but is also very caring and is liked by the AEUG pilots Kamille Bidan and Fa Yuiry.
She works hard at her job while also showing emotional support for the younger members.
Because of her near-death experiences in the past, she now has a taste for risk, and often satisfies it by going into deadly battles.
She is unfulfilled as a woman, but Quattro Bajeena and the other men of the AEUG take no heed of her feelings, and she becomes increasingly frustrated with them.

A girl from Side 7's Green Noa 1 colony. 
She has looked after her childhood friend, Kamille Bidan, since he was a young boy.
When the AEUG stole the Gundam Mk-II, Kamille was suspected of being involved, and her parents were arrested by association.
The Titans were about to arrest her as well when she was saved by Bright Noa, captain of the Temptation. She was then reunited with Kamille aboard the AEUG warship Argama.
She later volunteered as an AEUG pilot in order to stay close to Kamille and assist him as much as possible. She was assigned to pilot the Argama's MS Methuss, and fought in many battles.
She has a kind and caring personality, and likes looking after Kamille, as well as the young orphans Shinta and Qum who live aboard the Argama.

A girl from Side 7's Green Noa 1 colony. 
She has looked after her childhood friend, Kamille Bidan, since he was a young boy.
When the AEUG stole the Gundam Mk-II, Kamille was suspected of being involved, and her parents were arrested by association.
The Titans were about to arrest her as well when she was saved by Bright Noa, captain of the Temptation. She was then reunited with Kamille aboard the AEUG warship Argama.
She later volunteered as an AEUG pilot in order to stay close to Kamille and assist him as much as possible. She was assigned to pilot the Argama's MS Methuss, and fought in many battles.
She has a kind and caring personality, and likes looking after Kamille, as well as the young orphans Shinta and Qum who live aboard the Argama.

Captain of the AEUG warship Argama, and the former captain of the White Base.
Despite his achievements during the One Year War, he was given the cold shoulder by the mainstream Federation Forces, and relegated to commanding the orbital shuttle Temptation.
But after witnessing the violent actions of the Federation's elite Titans special forces at Green Noa 1, he decided to join the AEUG when the Temptation was rescued by the Argama.
He was a famed captain during the One Year War, and although he has been away from the front lines for the last seven years, he hasn't lost his skills.
He exhibits these effortlessly as captain of the Argama, becoming a central figure within the AEUG.
Just as in his White Base days, he is a military father figure who scolds and commands his crew, giving out punitive "corrections" for anyone who dares to disobey him.

An AEUG mobile suit pilot. She is a former member of the Federation's elite Titans special forces. She used to head into operations with the best of intentions, but gradually she grew to question the violent methods of her commander Bask Om, and eventually decided to join the AEUG.
She has high moral standards and behaves in a military manner, but at heart she is a kind and intelligent woman.
As a former Titans member she was put under watch at first, but gradually won the trust of the others as she fought alongside them. Eventually she became a central member of the MS pilot team.
As a former Federation Forces officer cadet, she teaches the youngsters Kamille Bidan, Fa Yuiry, and Katz Kobayashi about military protocol and offers them psychological support.

An orphan who traveled aboard the Federation Forces warship White Base during the One Year War. 
He decided to join the AEUG after meeting Amuro Ray, a hero of the One Year War, with his mother Fraw Kobayashi.
Because he is so young and immature, he is not very good at controlling his emotions, and often offends his superiors with his rude behavior.
He has grown up watching Amuro Ray, so he idolizes MS pilots and desperately wants to be a hero himself.
He is also competitive and often acts alone and messes up, but he gradually starts to grow out of this as he learns the grim reality of war.

Captain of the AEUG warship Radish. He is a soldier who has dedicated his life to the AEUG out of his indignation towards the Federation's elite Titans special forces.
He has a valiant yet combative personality, and often behaves violently. But he has a tactical eye and is sensitive to the needs of his subordinates, which makes him popular.
The AEUG leader Blex Forer greatly relies on him. In contrast to his bold persona as a soldier, he is very shy with women and tries desperately to catch the attention of Emma Sheen, clumsily coming up with new ways to approach her.
He was the first captain of the AEUG warship Argama, but was succeeded by the former White Base captain Bright Noa.

An MS pilot and a hero of the One Year War, who was feared by the Federation Forces because he demonstrated the potential of Newtypes too clearly.
Nominally employed at the Cheyenne base in North America, he was essentially under house arrest for his abilities.
The constant government surveillance and restrictions to his movements and lifestyle wore down his will to resist, and he fell into a spiritless, unmotivated rut. However, after a visit from his old friend Fraw Kobayashi and her children, he decides to join Karaba, an Earth-based sister organization to the AEUG, and meets with Katz Kobayashi.
After joining Karaba and getting to know the AEUG's Quattro Bajeena, Kamille Bidan, and Beltorchika Irma, he slowly but surely starts to regain his identity as a hero, and becomes an inspiration for young pilots such as Kamille Bidan.
Even after becoming an MS pilot he still fears going into space, instead preferring to fulfill his duties on Earth.

A central figure within the AEUG's Earth-based Karaba support group. He was an MS pilot for the Federation Forces warship White Base during the One Year War.
After the One Year War he remained part of the Federation Forces, but was feared as a potential Newtype, and relegated to working in a museum.
After sensing problems with the Federation Forces and their creation of the Titans, he decided to join Karaba. He is no longer the immature soldier he was during the One Year War, and leads his forces with calmness and dignity.
By lending his support from Earth, he is able to help members of the AEUG such as Kamille Bidan and Quattro Bajeena.

An MS pilot of the Federation's elite Titans special forces.
He is an ambitious man who hopes to take charge of the group.
He is an elitist and takes a lot of pride in being selected to be part of the Titans, making him feel superior to all others. But he also strives to learn, and when others question his narrow-mindedness, he tries to learn from them with great sincerity.
He has despised the AEUG pilot Kamille Bidan ever since they first met at Green Noa 1. He feels driven to surpass Kamille so that he can become stronger himself. This powerful urge makes him work harder.

An MS pilot of the Federation's elite Titans special forces.
She is the partner of fellow MS pilot Jerid Messa, and is always by his side.
Although she is usually quiet, when she feels something is wrong she will tell even her superiors to their face without any hesitation.
She has the MS piloting skills to match Jerid, and is aware of her surroundings on the battlefield, making her an exceptional soldier capable of analyzing battle. She loves Jerid from the bottom of her heart and will stay by his side even if it means risking her life.

An MS pilot of the Federation's elite Titans special forces.
He is a typical Titans officer who throws his weight around and never doubts the morality of the group.
He is proud to have been chosen as part of the elite Titans, and thus has a superior attitude towards others.
But he has good leadership skills during battle, and is a multi-talented MS pilot capable of taking on different roles. 
He is a good friend of the Titans pilot Jerid Messa, and understands his often torrid personality.

Acting commander of the Federation's 1st Orbital Fleet, and the person responsible for the deployment and activation of the Solar System II.
He ignores his aides and uses personal reasons to annihilate entire allied fleets, among other dastardly and un-soldier-like actions.
He is also an Earth supremacist and holds a heavy grudge against Spacenoids.
At the end of the Delaz Conflict, he is given command over the newly established elite Titans special forces.

A staff officer of the Federation's elite Titans special forces. He is a trusted subordinate of his commander Bask Om, and an arrogant commander typical of the Titans.
He is a good soldier and strategist who shows excellent leadership skills on the front lines, but also an elitist who considers himself superior to others.
He is oppressive towards subordinates but obedient to superiors, making him unpopular with his comrades.

Captain of the Titans warship Alexandria. He is a soldier with a sharp sensibility, and also an excellent captain.
He is a warrior who leads with a calm and patient attitude, while his abilities and energy truly exemplify the word "elite."
He is also a central figure within the Titans fleet.

Captain of the resource mining ship Jupitris, which shuttles between the Earth and the Jupiter Sphere. The "Man from Jupiter" brings ambition and great talent to the Titans.
He is an exceptional Newtype, a first-class pilot, an MS designer, and a skilled strategist. Aware of his many talents, he feels superior to others and has a tendency to look down on them, dubbing himself a "genius."
His experience in the Jupiter Sphere gave him a particular set of values, and he believes that women should be central to the governance of the Earth Sphere. It is thought that he surrounds himself with many women for this reason.
During the Gryps Conflict he took control of the Titans, but his intention was to manifest his own vision of the universe.

An MS pilot of the Federation's elite Titans special forces.
She is a subordinate to Paptimus Scirocco, captain of the Jupitris.
Drawn to Scirocco's charisma and noble ambitions for the future of the Earth Sphere, she worships him to the point where she would follow any order, however dangerous.
She also has Newtype abilities and is able to sense the will of others on the battlefield, but this often leads her to cave in to their pressure.

An MS pilot of the Federation's elite Titans special forces.
He is also the leader of the Yazan Team, a formation of three Hambrabi mobile suits.
An ace among the Titans pilots, he has the skills to rival Newtypes.
His ferocious battle style has earned him the nickname of "Beast," but he also has incredible intuition on the battlefield, making him an excellent leader.
He has a rugged appearance and ignores military protocol at will, making him unpopular with his superiors. He fights for the thrill and eliminates anyone he considers a nuisance, whether they are friend or foe.

An MS pilot of the Federation's elite Titans special forces.
He is a member of the Yazan Team made up of three Hambrabi mobile suits, and a subordinate to Yazan Gable.
Like his superior Yazan, he is low on morality but has good skills as a pilot, and under Yazan's command he performs synchronized battle tactics such as the Spider Web.

An MS pilot of the Federation's elite Titans special forces.
He is a member of the Yazan Team made up of three Hambrabi mobile suits, and a subordinate to Yazan Gable.
He would happily break military protocol on Yazan's orders, but he has good skills as a pilot, and under Yazan's command he performs synchronized battle tactics such as the Spider Web.

Leader of the mobile suit team aboard the warship Bosnia, based at the Federation Forces asteroid base Luna II.
She has a strong personality and great confidence in her abilities as a soldier. She also harbors hope that Jerid Messa, a pilot of the Federation's elite Titans special forces, will grow to be a real man one day.
Her skills as an MS pilot are top class and she even taught Jerid, who worshipped her as a master.
She is also a pure-blooded Federation Forces soldier who dislikes the elitist Titans.

A Cyber-Newtype who underwent conditioning at the Federation Forces' Augusta Laboratory.
She has phenomenal reaction speed and intuition, and can easily destroy an entire MS corps on her own. 
Her body and psyche were artificially adjusted in order to make her into a Newtype weapon.
She has superhuman MS piloting skills, but the manipulation of her trauma and fear of colony destruction to prepare her for battle has left her extremely psychologically unstable.

Commander of the Federation Forces airlifter Sudley. 
He has great confidence in his own abilities, and has the skills to match.
He is a pilot and soldier with a good tactical eye.
He is as daring and fearless as he looks, and is popular among his comrades for his accurate judgment and leadership.
He is also a pure-blooded Federation Forces soldier, and dislikes the elitist Titans.
However he also dislikes Spacenoids, and discriminates against the AEUG, calling them "aliens."

Captain of the Federation Forces airlifter Sudori. He is a confidant to the Federation Forces officer Buran Blutarch.
He lends his full support to Buran on the battlefield by giving him battle updates. He also does anything necessary to get the job done, even resorting to inhumane tactics such as taking hostages.
But he is popular with his comrades and many agree with his views. He is also a pure-blooded Federation Forces soldier, and dislikes the elitist Titans.
However, he has to obey orders from above and cooperate with them, participating in the pursuit of the Karaba airlifter Audhumla.

A Cyber-Newtype who underwent conditioning at the Federation Forces' Murasame Laboratory. She has phenomenal reaction speed and intuition, and can easily destroy an entire MS corps or warship on her own.
Her body and psyche were adjusted in order to make her into a Newtype weapon. She has superhuman MS piloting skills, but she was psychologically coerced into fighting, with her erased memories held hostage for her cooperation. 
This has damaged her psyche, leaving her extremely unstable.
She met AEUG MS pilot Kamille Bidan in New Hong Kong and they felt drawn to each other because of their similarities.
But her feelings towards Kamille, and her need to remember the past, end up tormenting her greatly.
The name "Four" was given to her because she was the fourth test subject of the Murasame Laboratory.

A Federation Forces technical officer. He is in charge of the development of the Gundam Mk-II.
He is also the father of AEUG pilot Kamille Bidan. He is a selfish man who only thinks and acts for himself.
Ambitious and technically-minded, he took on a lover despite having a family.

Regent of Axis and guardian of Mineva Lao Zabi.
She is essentially the leader of Axis.
She has exceptional Newtype abilities, and her phenomenal power pressures opponents. 
She finds the Newtype phenomenon of sharing memories disconcerting, and prefers to keep her distance. 
This may also come from her past when she was rejected by the Zeon pilot Char Aznable.
She dislikes those who meddle in her business and calls them "cretins." She is a far cry from typical Newtypes who have mutual understanding of others.
However, she is a skilled strategist, and during the conflict between the anti-Federation AEUG and the Federation's elite Titans special forces, she leads the Zeon remnants of Axis back to the Earth Sphere to restore the Zabi family. 
She continues to use the AEUG versus Titans conflict to keep herself in power.

An active boy who made his living by working as a junk dealer with his friends at the Side 1 colony Shangri-La.
He tried to steal the Zeta Gundam from the Argama when it docked at Shangri-La. After many twists and turns, he became an MS pilot for the AEUG, fighting many battles against Neo Zeon.
At first he fought because he didn't have anything better to do, but during his battles with Neo Zeon, he eventually found a reason to fight. He showed promise different from that of the Gundam pilots Amuro Ray and Kamille Bidan, opening the path for the next generation of Newtypes.

A volunteer soldier who joined the Anti Earth Union Group(AEUG), believing them to fight for justice. Because she is a regular soldier and is older than the others, she can come across as patronizing.
This caused friction with her comrades, including the ZZ Gundam pilot Judau Ashta and the Shangri-La kids, but eventually they all learned to get along and she joined the Argama's Gundam Team.

This boy lived through turbulent times with courage and tenacity as the leader of a group of young junk dealers in the Side 1 colony Shangri-La.
He appears to have an inferiority complex which has caused many clashes with the ZZ Gundam pilot Judau Ashta, but during the battle with Neo Zeon, he showed enough growth to be made acting captain of the Nahel Argama.

An exceptionally clever and quick-witted girl who was part of a group of young junk dealers in the Side 1 colony Shangri-La.
She was in charge of gathering information, until the Argama docked at Shangri-La and she was caught up in an incident involving the Zeta Gundam.
After many twists and turns, she joined the Argama's MS Gundam Team, using her unyielding personality and energy to act as a proxy leader when Judau Ashta and Beecha Oleg were unavailable.

A kind and sincere boy who was part of a group of young junk dealers in the Side 1 colony Shangri-La, he looks out for his team and is also quick to act.
Of all the troublemaking young junk dealers, he is the most serious and studious, and can fill many roles such as bridge crew, mechanic, MS pilot, and even crossdresser.

Part of a gang of young junk dealers in the Side 1 colony Shangri-La, this boy is good with electronics and is in charge of mecha maintenance.
Back in the day, he used to get into trouble with his friend Beecha Oleg, but grew up significantly after meeting and then parting with Rasara Moon of the Moon Moon colony.

Regent of Neo Zeon and guardian of Mineva Lao Zabi.
She is essentially the leader of Neo Zeon. Despite this, she has a hard time opening up to others, instead preferring solitude. This makes her lonely, but without anyone who shares her ideals, she continues to work towards her goal alone.

Commander of the Neo Zeon warship Endra. 
He worships Neo Zeon leader Haman Karn, and has pledged unwavering allegiance to her.
He is a young commander who has endless respect for the chivalrous code, but has a tendency toward narcissism that  ultimately compromises his battle skills.
He loves and respects Haman Karn so much that he gilded a rose she gave to him, and keeps it pinned to his chest.

An aide to commander Mashymre Cello of the Neo Zeon warship Endra II. She has strong skills as an MS pilot and even shows signs of being a Newtype.
Her real mission, by order of Neo Zeon leader Haman Karn, is to secretly monitor the Cyber-Newtype Mashymre, and manipulate him as a loyal and dedicated pawn to Haman's cause.

An aide to commander Mashymre Cello of the Neo Zeon warship Endra, as well as his successor Chara Soon.
He does his job diligently and has great talent, but has never been blessed with a good boss, often having to clean up after the inflexible Mashymre and wild Chara.

The leader of Neo Zeon's "3-D" Jamru Fin team, assigned to the Endra II.
He is a capable pilot who can make accurate assessments of situations.
Together with his 3-D comrades Dune and Del, he excels at 3-in-1 formation attacks.

A member of Neo Zeon's "3-D" Jamru Fin team, assigned to the Endra II.
He is an ambitious pilot with the abilities to match.
Together with his 3-D comrades Dune and Danny, he excels at 3-in-1 formation attacks.

A member of Neo Zeon's "3-D" Jamru Fin team, assigned to the Endra II.
He has a weakness for women, but is a highly capable pilot.
Together with his 3-D comrades Danny and Del, he excels at 3-in-1 formation attacks.

An officer placed aboard the warship Endra by Neo Zeon leader Haman Karn, tasked with monitoring the ship's blundering commander Mashymre Cello. She then later replaced Mashymre as commander of the Endra.
She tends to get extremely excited when piloting mobile suits, yelling wildly and taking action without cooperating with her comrades. This made her a nuisance to both friend and foe, but as she became aware of this herself, she started avoiding piloting MS whenever possible.

An MS pilot in Neo Zeon's Royal Guard. He is the twin brother of Nee Gylen, who is also a Royal Guard member.
He was chosen by Haman Karn, the leader of Neo Zeon, to escort and supervise the Cyber-Newtype commander Chara Soon.
He fulfilled his duties while piloting a red Gaz-L mobile suit.

An MS pilot in Neo Zeon's Royal Guard. He is the twin brother of Lance Gylen, who is also a Royal Guard member.
He was chosen by Haman Karn, the leader of Neo Zeon, to escort and supervise the Cyber-Newtype commander Chara Soon.
He fulfilled his duties while piloting a blue Gaz-R mobile suit.

Initially, he was just a soldier Haman had her eye on, and was assigned as a pilot under Mashymre Cello, commander of the battleship Endra.
However, his talents developed over time, and while he outwardly supported Haman, he assembled a Newtype Corps behind her back. He was even able to convince the spy sent to watch him to join his own side.
After thus laying the groundwork for a rebellion, he eventually seized control of Axis from Haman.

Her Newtype abilities allow her to operate the psycommu-equipped Qubeley Mk-II, and are well suited to piloting tricky machines such as the ZZ Gundam and the other MS aboard the Argama.
She generally has a pure heart and behaves in a naive and immature way, due to her young age.
She met the ZZ Gundam pilot Judau Ashta when he sneaked into Axis. She became fond of him, following him around even though he was an enemy. After many twists and turns, she joins the Argama and starts living with Judau and his comrades, saving them on many occasions.

A subordinate of Neo Zeon officer Glemy Toto, and the leader
of his Newtype Corps. She is the first of the "Ple Series" of clone Newtypes.
She is a clone and twin of the Newtype girl Elpeo Ple, and due to later adjustments she harbors only the most vicious aspects of Ple's personality. Because of this she has a cold and combative personality, unlike Ple.

The commander of the six-man "Space Wolf Team" reporting to Neo Zeon officer Glemy Toto. He is a veteran warrior who has been fighting since the One Year War.
He has absolute confidence in his abilities, and his piloting skills rival those of Cyber-Newtypes.
He prides himself on having never been taken down while piloting a mobile suit, and therefore does not wear a normal suit during battle.

A former Titans MS pilot, who narrowly escaped death when his machine was destroyed at the end of the Gryps Conflict, and was then rescued by Judau Ashta of the Side 1 colony Shangri-La.
He has a brutal side that has earned him the nickname of "Beast." When he heard that the AEUG's Argama had docked at Shangri-La, he and Judau took the Argama crew as hostages in an attempt to steal the Zeta Gundam.

The boss of the junk dealer union at the Side 1 colony Shangri-La, he is a simple man at heart, but is disliked by many for his arrogance.
When Neo Zeon's Endra Team entered Shangri-La, he offered his cooperation and then ran wild with his custom-made mobile worker Geze, displaying a staggering lack of self-awareness about his own capabilities.

Appeared in Mobile Suit Gundam 0083: Stardust Memory during the One Year War.
He was Captain of the 302nd Patrol Squadron of the Space Strike Fleet commanded by Dozle Zabi.
During the Battle of Solomon, he fought valiantly as the rear guard while his allies retreated.
He inflicted serious damage on the pursuing Federation Forces fleet, earning the nickname the Nightmare of Solomon.

A member of the Federation Survey Service and a skilled mobile suit pilot.
His memory is a bit spotty, and some suspect he is Johnny Ridden, an ace pilot during the One Year War.

Commander of the mobile suit forces of the Federation's Londo Bell task force. He is a Federation Forces ace pilot who has endured many battles since the One Year War.
His age has given him calmness, and the dark side seen during the Gryps Conflict has now all but worn off.
He is also considerate and measured in his actions, and has high battle skills and good intuition. This has made him a much better MS pilot and person.
He has a strong animosity towards his rival Char Aznable, leader of the reborn Neo Zeon, but he has decided to put an end to it with this battle.

Commander of the mobile suit forces of the Federation's Londo Bell task force. He is a Federation Forces ace pilot who has endured many battles since the One Year War.
His age has given him calmness, and the dark side seen during the Gryps Conflict has now all but worn off.

He has a strong animosity towards his rival Char Aznable, leader of the reborn Neo Zeon, but he has decided to put an end to it with this battle.

He has a strong animosity towards his rival Char Aznable, leader of the reborn Neo Zeon, but he has decided to put an end to it with this battle.

Supreme leader of the reborn Neo Zeon forces. He is an ace pilot also known as the "Red Comet."
He is the son of the Zeon founder Zeon Zum Deikun, and plans to purge the people who have remained behind to contaminate the Earth, thus forcing humanity to reform into Newtypes.
During the Gryps Conflict, he fought under the name Quattro Bajeena and tried to reform humanity.
But after seeing how corrupt the Federation government and the other people of Earth were, he decided to take drastic action, reviving Neo Zeon and planning Earth's destruction by freezing it with a nuclear winter.
He has a long history with his arch-nemesis, Londo Bell ace pilot Amuro Ray, but he hopes that this war will put an end to their rivalry.

Supreme leader of the reborn Neo Zeon forces. He is an ace pilot also known as the "Red Comet."
He is the son of the Zeon founder Zeon Zum Deikun, and plans to purge the people who have remained behind to contaminate the Earth, thus forcing humanity to reform into Newtypes.

He has a long history with his arch-nemesis, Londo Bell ace pilot Amuro Ray, but he hopes that this war will put an end to their rivalry.

Commander of the Federation Forces' Londo Bell task force.
He is also a veteran soldier and captain of its flagship Ra
Cailum.
He has captained many ships including the White Base, and
even after being made commander of Londo Bell, he leads with
a resolute attitude. He is a reliable and experienced
leader, and is popular with his comrades.
He has full trust in his comrades and the mobile suit pilot
Amuro Ray, and always asks his opinion when coming up with
formations and operation plans for his forces.

An MS pilot of the Federation Forces' Londo Bell task force.
She is a central figure in its mobile suit forces, with ability second only to Amuro Ray.
She has a brave personality and is serious, with a strong sense of responsibility.
But occasionally this works against her, and blinds her to the big picture.
However, the moment she leaves the battlefield, she changes into a bright and happy person, especially when with her lover and Londo Bell chief mechanic, Astonaige Medoz.

Son of the Londo Bell commander Bright Noa. 
He is highly sensitive and very naive, but has a shrewd side that understands how adults perceive youngsters like him. He also has a tendency to over-think and can be stubborn.
When Quess Paraya, daughter of a Federation official, joins Neo Zeon, he stows away aboard the Londo Bell flagship Ra Cailum to get her back. He acts on his emotions, not listening to his father Bright Noa or the Londo Bell ace pilot Amuro Ray.

A technical officer of the Federation Forces' Londo Bell task force. She is a talented mechanic responsible for developing and maintaining the task force's mobile suits.
She is a serious soldier who nurses a crush on the Londo Bell ace pilot Amuro Ray, but never reveals her feelings during operations. Instead she prefers to separate their private and public lives and support him across all areas.
While she is primarily involved with the development and maintenance of the new mobile suit Nu Gundam, she is an active woman who will take up the position of gunner when necessary, and once even went out into the battlefield with a psycho-frame to assist Amuro.

Daughter of the Federation official Adenauer Paraya.
She is a highly sensitive and observant Newtype.
Her turbulent emotions and selfishness prevent her from properly grasping and considering situations. She also lacks the skills to express herself, and often clashes with the people around her. But her ability to read other people's
feelings is strong, and this leads to her decision to fight alongside Char after sensing his true intent.
After joining the Neo Zeon forces, she shows promise as a Newtype MS pilot. But she too often allows her emotions to affect her combat, and struggles to cooperate with others.
She also lacks good judgment on the battlefield and is easily defeated by the Londo Bell pilot Amuro Ray.

Daughter of the Federation official Adenauer Paraya.

An MS pilot aboard the Neo Zeon flagship Rewloola.
He is an ambitious Cyber-Newtype who was enhanced by the Newtype Laboratory.
He is overconfident and calls himself a true Newtype, but does have MS piloting skills and psycommu control abilities that match those of most Newtypes. Even so, he is drawn to Quess Paraya, whose Newtype skills easily surpass his own.
But Quess is committed to the Neo Zeon leader Char Aznable, and does not even look at Gyunei. He also has trouble accepting that Char is using Quess's abilities for his own benefit, and so he pretends to be loyal to Char while secretly hoping to overthrow him.

Leader of a Geara Doga team aboard the Neo Zeon flagship Rewloola.
She has skills that surpass most others within the Neo Zeon forces.
She is highly confident and has an irreverent personality, but fights with a good balance of boldness and precision.
She has an impeccable ability to determine when to withdraw, and is an excellent pilot who has the abilities to match her courageous attitude.
But because she is an Oldtype, she detests Newtypes and Cyber-Newtypes, and believes that whether Newtype or not, only one's abilities on the battlefield matter. 
For this reason she always tries to compete with the Cyber-Newtype Gyunei Guss and Newtype Quess Paraya.

A student at the colony Industrial 7, who attends the Anaheim Electronics Industrial College.
He leads a normal life while seeking answers to bigger questions, until he meets the mysterious Audrey Burne and becomes caught up in the conflict surrounding Laplace's Box.
He has a calm demeanor that belies his youth, and does not show his emotions very often. He also rarely takes action unless forced, but is greatly moved by his chance meeting with Audrey Burne and her wish to stop the war, so he decides to help her out.
He then meets his biological father Cardeas Vist in the colony-turned-battlefield and is given the Unicorn Gundam, which turns out to be the key to Laplace's Box. This draws him further and further into the war.

An MS pilot assigned to the warship Nahel Argama of the Federation's Londo Bell task force.
He is also the heir to the Marcenas political family.
He is a young soldier who chose to follow his dream as a pilot after feeling suffocated by the pretensions of his upbringing.
He is usually a bright and happy man who is always at ease, but his youth makes him reckless and thoughtless of the consequences his behavior might create.
Because of his lineage he often gets special treatment, and he hates this.
But through meeting the orphaned Mineva Lao Zabi, he learns the significance of Laplace's Box and is gradually caught up in the incident.

Ringleader of the Neo Zeon remnant group known as the Sleeves.
He is a masked man also known as the "Second Coming of Char Aznable."
He has cold and accurate judgment, and charisma capable of gathering the scattered Neo Zeon remnants into a powerful force.
He has many die-hard believers, but he rarely shows his true feelings, and has no problem discarding his comrades to achieve his goals.
Like Char Aznable, he has high skills as an MS pilot.
On the battlefield, he shows his true power by holding his own against the Unicorn Gundam, even with its NT-D system activated.

The twelfth clone Newtype of the Ple Series, created by the Neo Zeon officer Glemy Toto as part of his Newtype Corps.
Although originally an MS pilot with the Neo Zeon remnant group known as the Sleeves, she was captured by the Nahel Argama and turned over to Martha Vist Carbine to be reconditioned as a Cyber-Newtype.
Because of this, her memories are suppressed and she is left without emotion, turning into a coldhearted weapon carrying out the orders of her new master, Alberto Vist.

An MS pilot of the Neo Zeon remnant group known as the Sleeves.
He is a powerful young man capable of supporting ringleader Full Frontal as commander of his Guards.
Because Full Frontal saved him from a terrible situation, he has full-hearted trust in the man, and is prepared to follow any orders given to him, however underhanded the operation.
He usually keeps a calm demeanor, but when it comes to Full Frontal, he will become incredibly emotional and show his raw feelings.
To Angelo Sauper, who had once lost all hope, Full Frontal was his everything.

A Cyber-Newtype MS pilot who belongs to the Neo Zeon remnant group known as the Sleeves.
She is a member of the talented Garencieres Team.
She never usually shows her feelings, and behaves coolly to all she meets.
But when Banagher Links feels guilty for killing on the battlefield, she comforts him and shows her compassionate side.
She is a strong-willed woman, with a terrible past due to her unique origins.
She is therefore fiercely loyal to Suberoa Zinnerman, who saved her and gave her a new name.

Commander of the Federation's ECOAS 920 special operations team, who is proud to be a cog in the organization.
He is a brash and unrefined soldier who completes all tasks assigned to him.
Because they conduct shady missions in the name of anti- terrorism, ECOAS are often referred to as the "Manhunters," and he lives up to this name with his cold and stubborn persona.
However, deep inside he is peaceful and wants nothing more than to fulfill his duties, even if it regrettably means resorting to underhanded tactics.

He is a brash and unrefined soldier who completes all tasks assigned to him.

However, deep inside he is peaceful and wants nothing more than to fulfill his duties, even if it regrettably means resorting to underhanded tactics.

An MS pilot of the Londo Bell task force. 
He is the leader of the flagship Ra Cailum's ace pilot team, the "Tri-Stars."
He has a cool demeanor and gentlemanly manner, but is confident enough to criticize his superiors.
His skills as a pilot are excellent, and his accuracy during team maneuvers is highly rated within the Federation Forces.
His superior skills made him a test pilot candidate for the UC project, but after the project was cancelled, he was assigned to test the Unicorn Gundam's support MS Jesta.

An MS pilot of the Londo Bell task force.
He is a member of the flagship Ra Cailum's ace pilot team, the "Tri-Stars."
He may seem aloof, but he has a dangerous side to him.

His accuracy during team maneuvers is highly rated within the Federation Forces.

An MS pilot of the Londo Bell task force.

A member of the talented Garencieres Team, part of the Neo Zeon remnant group known as the Sleeves.
He is a mobile suit pilot who also takes the role of ship's helmsman.
He is a talkative and friendly man, with a wife and three children living at the Sleeves' Palau base.

Pilot of the huge mobile armor Shamblo.
She is a member of the the Zeon remnant group led by Yonem Kirks.
She is the daughter of Mahdi Garvey, a wealthy man who helped bring the remnants together.
The loss of her parents at the hands of the Federation Forces filled her with unspeakable rage, and she yearns for a chance to avenge their deaths.

An MS pilot, and current leader of the Zeon remnants based in New Guinea. 
A gritty, professional soldier, he is like a father to his subordinates.
He has earned the absolute confidence of Loni Garvey, and during the Dakar raid carried out at the request of the Sleeves, he showed that he has what it takes to lead.

Mobile suit team commander of the warship Nahel Argama of the Federation's Londo Bell task force.
He operates under the code name "Romeo 1."
He is a veteran pilot with honed MS piloting skills, and although he is strict with his subordinates, he cares for them greatly and risks his life to protect them, making him a very popular leader.

The secretary of current Vist Foundation leader Cardeas Vist.
This muscular man also serves as a bodyguard and MS pilot.
Originally a member of the Federation Forces, he sympathizes with Cardeas Vist's vision of building a new world in a future where the Box has been opened, and becomes his trusted confidant.
Though he is a silent type who rarely shows emotion, the mutual trust between him and Cardeas Vist goes beyond that of the typical employer-employee relationship.
When Full Frontal attacks Magallanica, he risks his life in battle to clear a path for Cardeas Vist's son, Banagher Links.

In U.C. 0095, the Unicorn Gundam 03 Phenex went out of control and subsequently disappeared.
Jona Basta, piloting the Narrative Gundam, reinforced the specially formed Shezarr Team during the Phoenix Hunt, a mission to find the missing mobile suit.
He is a childhood friend of Michele Luio, a special advisor to Luio & Co., and Rita Bernal, the missing pilot of the Phenex.

His father is EFSF 13th Independent Fleet commander Bright Noa.
As a child, Hathaway lived through the Second Neo Zeon War caused by Char Aznable (Char's Rebellion). At present, he has completed a brief military service and is undergoing training as a botanical supervisor from professor Amada Mansan.
While returning from a colony's agricultural block on Haunzen Flight 356, he encounters hijackers claiming to be Mafty, but manages to repel them with the physical prowess from his military training, which earns him the praise of many cabinet members.
However, Hathaway is in fact Mafty Navue Erin, leader of the anti-Earth Federation movement Mafty, which intends to assassinate those very same cabinet members.

The test pilot of the new Gundam model, Penelope, which was sent to the Mafty Annihilation Unit (later known as the Circe Unit) before Kenneth took command.
His rank is lieutenant junior grade.
Despite his young age, he is an elite pilot entrusted with a unit, but loses respect from Kenneth for acting self-assured due to his high scores as a test pilot who hasn't taken part in actual combat.

A young man serving as a pilot for Mafty, with a strong inclination toward training.
His pilot call sign is Messer 3.

A member of Mafty and veteran pilot who has survived countless battles.
He is easily identified by the large scar on his face.
His pilot call sign is Messer 2.

A member of Mafty and veteran pilot who has survived countless battles.

A girl whose stunning good looks draw the admiration of men and envy of women aboard Haunzen Flight 356, a shuttle to Hong Kong.
Why she is headed to Hong Kong and where she came from are a mystery, but her elegant demeanor and her presence on the Haunzen suggest that she is in the good graces of a powerful patron.
Not caring to play along with the artifices of adult society, she speaks her mind frankly. When men are faced with this behavior, their concealed thoughts start sprouting out, and even Hathaway's past, frozen into hard ice, begins to thaw.

His rank is captain.

In response to the increasing radicalization of the anti-Earth Federation movement Mafty, he is assigned to the Davao air base overseeing the South Pacific, to take over Kimberly Hayman's command of the Mafty Annihilation Unit.
He meets Hathaway aboard Haunzen Flight 356, the two sharing the same idea of what a pilot should be.

The big-sister figure among the Mafty members. She was sent to Davao to rendezvous with Hathaway.
In her past, she was unable to become an official mobile suit pilot due to her lack of technique.

The big-sister figure among the Mafty members. She was sent to Davao to rendezvous with Hathaway.

Captain of the First Galcezon. Mafty member.
He is a big man with an imposing build, but has a kind and considerate side for his companions.

This young woman is a skilled mechanic who is entrusted with maintaining Hathaway Noa's personal Mobile Suit, the Xi Gundam.
She likes to wear revealing clothing.

A test pilot for S.N.R.I.
He was enlisted in the Federation Forces to perform operational tests on the Gundam F90 and piloted Unit 1.
However, he was attacked by the Mars Zeon Independence Army during a test flight, and he was assigned to the Federation fleet to take part in the retaliatory mission.
On Mars, he engaged with Unit 2 and defeated it, returning home in one piece.

A young man in the resistance.
He is the protagonist of Mobile Suit Gundam F91.
He was an ordinary high school student living on the newly established colony Frontier IV until the Crossbone Vanguard attacked and he escaped with his friends.
Afterwards, he took refuge aboard the training ship Space Ark, the base of the resistance, and became the pilot of its prototype mobile suit Gundam F91.

A pilot for the new Crossbone Vanguard.
He pilots the Crossbone Gundam X1 as the new Crossbone Vanguard's young ace pilot.
In truth, he is Seabook Arno, former pilot of the Gundam F91.
When Cecily Fairchild began a resistance movement against the Jupiter Empire under the alias Berah Ronah, he joined the new Crossbone Vanguard under the alias Kincade Nau.

A Gundam Fighter representing Neo Japan in the 13th Gundam Fight.
He participated in the tournament in search of his brother, Kyoji Kasshu, who stole the Ultimate Gundam and escaped to Earth.
It was also the reason their father is sentenced to indefinite frozen imprisonment.
He is also a colonial martial arts champion and a disciple who inherited The School of Master Asia and King of Hearts, one of the Shuffle's emblems, from Master Asia, Undefeated of the East.
To achieve his goals, he took selfish actions without considering his surroundings and let his emotions get the better of him.
However, during his time in the Gundam Fight, he gained new friendships, overcame obstacles and learned from the tragedies that came one after another. He matured as both a Gundam Fighter and a human being.

He often appears before Domon Kasshu, encouraging and guiding him.
During the training at Guiana Highlands, he was the very person who made Domon understand the groundbreaking Mei Kyo Shisui.
Domon sees him as his second master.

She is a talented woman who handles everything from machine maintenance to intelligence operations.
Bright and devoted with a strong sense of responsibility, she is well-liked by everyone.
She is a childhood friend of Domon and always worries about him, as he often acts on his own.
Gradually, both of them learn to overcome the battles together and fall in love.
Despite not being an official Gundam Fighter, she pilots the Shining Gundam to fight in the event of an emergency.

He was once a Gundam Fighter who fought against the Dark Gundam.
Now, he dons a mask that covers half of his face, and much of his background is shrouded in mystery.
During Domon's Gundam Fights, he is present on-site, giving directions on Chairman Karato's behalf.

A Gundam Fighter representing Neo Hong Kong in the 13th Gundam Fight.
He is the winner of the 12th Gundam Fight, the creator of The School of Master Asia and owner of the King of Hearts before Domon Kasshu. His ability is staggering and has the superhuman strength to destroy a Mobile Fighter with his bare hands.
He is usually a gentle and calm person who loves art and poetry, but during fights, he sticks to his beliefs and crushes his opponent with cruel mercy.

A Gundam Fighter who represented Neo England.
With peerless marksmanship, he was a hero who once won three consecutive Gundam Fight championships.
Although he retired for a time, he threw himself back into fighting to reclaim past glory.
However, his body was already at its limit and was being maintained somehow by stimulants.

A Gundam Fighter representing Neo Nepal.
He is a blind man who specializes in taking down opponents quickly.
Although he used to be an outstanding Gundam Fighter, he was blinded in a bout and his since resorted to assassinating his opponents before the match.

A Gundam Fighter representing Neo Italy.
He was a mafia boss who controlled Rome, and he abused his privilege as a Gundam Fighter to cover his unconscionable behavior time and time again.
However, he was finally imprisoned for his past crimes after being utterly defeated by Domon Kasshu in his Shining Gundam.

A member of the Shuffle Alliance.
She was a woman who assumed leadership within the faction.
Her specialty was versatile combat with a chain.
She appeared to purge Master Asia, who sought to revive the Dark Gundam, but recognized in Argo Gulskii the potential to become her successor during battle and sacrificed herself to free him from his DG Cells.
Her favored Gundam was the Shuffle Joker.

A member of the Shuffle Alliance.
He was a man of Arab descent who wore a turban.
His specialty was throwing knives.
He appeared to purge Master Asia, who sought to revive the Dark Gundam, but recognized in Chibodee Crocket the potential to become his successor during battle and sacrificed himself to free him from his DG Cells.
His favored Gundam was the Shuffle Spade.

A member of the Shuffle Alliance.
He was a dignified man who smoked a pipe.
His specialty was throwing flames with his pipe.
He appeared to purge Master Asia, who sought to revive the Dark Gundam, but recognized in George de Sand the potential to become his successor during battle and sacrificed himself to free him from his DG Cells.
His favored Gundam was the Shuffle Diamond.

A member of the Shuffle Alliance.
He was a man of Indian descent who wore robes.
His specialty was martial arts.
He appeared to purge Master Asia, who sought to revive the Dark Gundam, but recognized in Sai Saici the potential to become his successor during battle and sacrificed himself to free him from his DG Cells.
His favored Gundam was the Shuffle Ace.

An engineer for Neo Japan.
He was developing the Ultimate Gundam with his father, Dr. Kasshu, but when the military attempted to seize it, he fled to Earth in the mobile suit before it was complete.

A Gundam Fighter representing Neo Sweden in the 13th Gundam Fight.
The only female Gundam Fighter in the Gundam Fight Finals.
Educated in a military facility since young, she specializes in military martial arts and fights with her flexible body.
Initially a free-spirited and friendly girl, she closes off her heart to everyone after being treated as an experimental subject for the Berserker System.
However, she opens up her heart again when she met Domon Kasshu under similar circumstances.
Her feelings for Domon is a mixture of romance and brotherly love.

However, she opens up her heart again when she met Domon Kasshu under similar circumstances.

A young agent working for the Colony Liberation Organization.
He was raised in the L1 Colony Cluster, where Doctor J gave him special training from a young age. 
He is extremely skilled as a special agent, a formidable Mobile Suit pilot and also possessing superhuman physical attributes.
Taciturn and blunt, he prioritizes the mission above all else, and would die for the mission without a second thought.
Once Operation Meteor was underway in the year AC 195, he made planetfall in the Wing Gundam and began acting against OZ as an undermining force.
The name "Heero Yuy" is a code name borrowed from a hero of the independence movement. His real name is unknown.

A young agent working for the Colony Liberation Organization.
He was raised in the L1 Colony Cluster, where Doctor J gave him special training from a young age. 
He is extremely skilled as a special agent, a formidable Mobile Suit pilot and also possessing superhuman physical attributes.

Once Operation Meteor was underway in the year AC 195, he made planetfall in the Wing Gundam and began acting against OZ as an undermining force.
The name ""Heero Yuy"" is a code name borrowed from a hero of the independence movement. His real name is unknown.

A young agent working for the Colony Liberation Organization.
He is a highly skilled fighter and agent trained by the Sweeper Group, a group of space nomads in the L2 Colony Cluster. 
He is a solid Mobile Suit pilot who calls himself the "Grim Reaper," and is right at home in his Gundam Deathscythe.
Personality-wise, he is upbeat and sociable, and the most down-to-earth of the five Gundam pilots.
Once Operation Meteor was underway in the year AC 195, he made planetfall in Gundam Deathscythe. 
He carries out his missions with support from Mike Howard, the captain of a salvage vessel.

A young agent working for the Colony Liberation Organization.
An agent from the L3 Colony Cluster, much about him is unknown. Even his name, Trowa Barton, is an alias. 
He has been a mercenary since his younger years, and is highly proficient in both Mobile Suit and on-foot combat.
He is a calm, collected boy who never gives away his emotions. He views himself as a tool of warfare and holds little regard for his own life.
He was initially a mechanic for the Gundam Heavyarms. When its original pilot, Trowa Barton, died, he took Trowa's place and became part of Operation Meteor. 
After making planetfall, he went undercover as a circus clown to carry out his mission.

A young agent working for the Colony Liberation Organization.
He is the heir to the prodigiously wealthy Winner family in the L4 Colony Cluster, and has a natural aptitude for Mobile Suit piloting and combat.
He is a sensitive boy with a kindhearted and modest personality and a knack for socializing. 
He acts as the figure that holds the group of Gundam pilots together.
In the year AC 195, he took Gundam Sandrock against the wishes of his war-averse father and took part in Operation Meteor. 
He joined forces with the Maganac Corps, an independent group of old acquaintances operating in the Middle East, and received their help to carry out his mission.

A young agent working for the Colony Liberation Organization.
He comes from the L5 Colony Cluster, where he was born into a family of Chinese warriors who could trace their roots back to ancient times, and was raised to be a first-rate martial artist. 
He puts his superb combat prowess to use both in hand-to-hand fights and Mobile Suit battles.
He is a proud and stoic boy with a firm and uncompromising personality. He believes strongly in fighting for justice, and shows no mercy to those he perceives as evil.
In the year AC 195, he volunteered to take part in Operation Meteor. He made planetfall in Shenlong Gundam, where he carried out his mission to great effect.

The commander of the Middle Eastern Maganac Corps.
He is a brave fighter and veteran leader in charge of 40 men.
He has sworn loyalty to Quatre Raberba Winner, and his Maganac Corps have fought numerous battles all over the world in his name.

OZ's ace pilot, AKA the Lightning Baron (later, Count).
He possesses a great aptitude for Mobile Suit piloting, as well as a calm decision-making style that earns him trust from Treize Khushrenada, Commander of OZ. He also exhibits great leadership that earns him solid trust from his men.
He always hides his face behind a mask. His true identity is Milliardo Peacecraft, prince of the Sanc Kingdom, a country that advocated pacifism and was subsequently destroyed by the United Earth Sphere Alliance.
Zechs insinuated himself into the Alliance in order to avenge his fallen land, and he achieved great distinction among the elite of OZ. 
His vengeance was fulfilled when OZ launched their coup d'etat, and the Sanc Kingdom was liberated from its previous occupation.

Zechs Merquise, after reverting to his true name.
After OZ's coup saw the Sanc Kingdom restored, Zechs took on his original name of Milliardo Peacecraft, and provided backing to his younger sister Relena by serving as his homeland's ambassador.
However, the Sanc Kingdom was destroyed a second time by the Romefeller Foundation. Quinze, leader of White Fang, sought him out while he was wallowing in despair and invited him to join them.
Once he became Commander of White Fang, he declared that he would wipe out the Earth in order to strike fear of war into the hearts of all mankind.

An OZ officer and pilot instructor working at Lake Victoria Base.
She comes from a line of Italian aristocrats, and has been seeing Zechs Merquise since their officer school days. 
She is a solid pilot with a keen wit and strong drive. In a time of war and chaos, she sticks to her guns and continues to love Zechs all throughout.
She lost her class of students when Chang Wufei launched an attack on Lake Victoria Base, at which point she started working alongside Zechs instead. 
After leaving OZ, Noin became a personal guard for Zechs' younger sister Relena Peacecraft, and assembled forces to help guard the Sanc Kingdom.
Once the newly reborn Sanc Kingdom was destroyed, she headed into space and worked with the Gundam pilots there up until the final battle. She then ran to Zechs' side, who was now Commander of White Fang, and watched over his battle.

An OZ officer posted at Corsica Base and an acquaintance of Zechs Merquise.
When Zechs visited Corsica Base, he entrusted Zechs with the Tallgeese that was mothballed there. 
The base came under attack by Gundams shortly afterward, and Walker sortied in an Aries, fully prepared to die in his quest for data acquisition.

The charismatic young Commander of OZ, and later Ruler of the World Nation.
He is a born elite with all the talent and charisma of a true genius. He believes strongly that the willingness to fight is, in and of itself, what stimulates growth for all of mankind. He acts according to his own personal philosophies and sense of aesthetics.
In the year AC 195, Treize launched a coup against the United Earth Sphere Alliance and overturned their rule.
OZ's parent organization, the Romefeller Foundation, aimed simply to rule the Earth Sphere utilizing Mobile Dolls.
When Treize voiced his objection to this idea, he was removed from power and imprisoned.
He withdrew from the stage of history after that, only to take advantage of the rise of White Fang and seize power within the organization once more. 
He faced off against White Fang as the leader of the World Nation's military.

The loyal right-hand woman of Treize Khushrenada, Commander of OZ.
She admired Treize greatly and devoted her life to making his ideals a reality. Sometimes she commits atrocities as a callous commanding officer, and other times she dons the mask of a saint to win the hearts of the colonies.
Her inability to grasp Treize's true intentions has led to her exhibiting symptoms of multiple personalities.
She was in command of the space fleet after OZ's coup.
When Treize got drummed out, she got shot and badly wounded by Tubarov. 
Later, when Treize made his comeback and sortied as Commander of the World Nation military, she recovered and rushed to the battlefield.

Relena Darlian, after taking on her original name.
When her father died in a terrorist attack orchestrated by OZ, Relena learned that she was Princess of the Sanc Kingdom.
She took on the royal family surname of Peacecraft and became Head of State for the newly-restored Sanc Kingdom.
She worked toward the ideal of total pacifism advocated by her late father and gained increasing numbers of supporters.
However, the Romefeller Foundation perceived this as a threat and launched a military intervention that led to the second downfall of the Sanc Kingdom.

A technical officer for OZ.
A technician under Tubarov, he was a man of ambition.
When OZ obtained the Wing Gundam Zero, he became intrigued by the Zero System it was equipped with and conducted research into it against Tubarov's orders.

A test pilot for the industrial colony MO-V.
He has a calm and collected demeanor, and is a highly-skilled pilot. He is also the older brother of fellow test pilot Odin.
He worked as a test pilot on MO-V for the prototype Mobile Suit Geminass 02, but got caught up in the fighting over the new Gundam model. He went MIA while protecting Odin during a battle with OZ Prize.

A trusted associate of Valder Farkill and a Mobile Suit pilot of OZ Prize.
She is the elder of two sisters; her younger sister is Luna Armonia.
They both lost their parents at a young age.
The colonial independence movement raised them to be special agents, but they did not make the cut when the Gundam pilots were selected. Valder Farkill recruited them after that.
Both sisters swore loyalty to Valder for saving them, and they have carried out numerous missions under his orders.

Former agent of the Colony Liberation Organization.
He is still the life of the party and frequent companion of Heero Yuy even a year after the cessation of hostilities between Earth's nations and the White Fang.
Though he helped send the Gundams to the Sun in hopes of bringing peace to the world, learning of Relena Darlian's abduction by the Mariemaia Army immediately afterward saw him join up with Heero to take the fight to the hostile colonies. 
He later dropped onto Mareimaia-occupied Brussels in his recovered Gundam Deathscythe Hell to fight alongside his former allies in a fierce battle.

The pilot of the Gundam X. 
He joins the Vulture as one of the Freeden crew to protect Tiffa Adill, the girl he fell in love with.
Once a war orphan, he lived a vulture-like life, scavenging for war scraps to make a living. 
During one of his odd jobs, he met a Newtype, Tiffa Adill, and the Gundam X, which lead him to the journey with Freeden.
Having grown up alone, living in a group is unfamiliar to Garrod. At first, he rebelled and was unable to trust anyone around him, but after going through many battles, Garrod learned to trust and grow as both a human being and a pilot.

The pilot of the Gundam X.

A Newtype girl with a mysterious ability to predict the future.
Born with the ability to see the future and the activation of the Flash System, her special power causes her to be deprived of her freedom and treated like a research subject since childhood.
This made her to be introverted and expressing little of herself. 
However, after meeting Garrod Ran and the crew, Tiffa slowly opens up the door to her heart and regains the real personality in her.

A mobile suit pilot aboard the Freeden.
Straightforward and hot-blooded, he can be rude but also reserved.
He pilots a mobile suit to provide a better life for his family and sends all his earnings back home.

A mobile suit pilot aboard the Freeden.
Straightforward and hot-blooded, he can be rude but also reserved.

A pilot aboard the Freeden.
A playboy through and through, he is easygoing and always plays it cool. He doesn't care for politics, and he avoids political and ideological confrontations.
Initially a freelancer, he eventually signed an exclusive contract with Freeden and threw himself into the fight against the New United Nations Earth.

Captain of the Vulture's land battleship, Freeden.
In the 7th Space War, he piloted the Gundam X as a Newtype soldier, but due to his guilt of being involved in the Colony Drop tragedy, he lost his Newtype ability and developed cockpit phobia.
After the war, he traveled around the world to rescue Newtypes used by the military.
He has a reserved and strict personality, and is a man of few words. 
He is an excellent leader who can gather members with strong personalities and an advisor for Garrod Ran, who still lacks experience.

The chief operator of the Freeden.
She is also second-in-command to Captain Jamil Neate and serves as acting captain in his absence.
She is a capable woman, calm and collected, but can be a little stern.

A freelance mobile suit pilot.
The daughter of Nada El, a Space Revolutionary Army soldier, and an Earth woman, she lost both of her parents as a child.
Ever since, she has survived the war-torn after-war world on her own.
Competitive and vindictive, she has harbored a grudge against Garrod Ran since he rejected her advances.

A Cyber Newtype who leads the Fort Severn city guards to protect the city.
Realizing that the chaotic world is run by selfish meritocrats who greed for more power, he blindly begins the pursuit of his ideal peaceful world.
An intelligent individual with a strong sense of justice, but because of his pure-hearted nature, he became the pawn of the mayor's selfish needs and plans.
The Cyber Newtype ability given to Carris Nautilus is not any different from the natural Newtype ability. However, there is a side effect where Cyber Newtypes suffer from the Synapse Syndrome, a strong headache causing extreme pain, which also can be life-threatening.

Gundam Virsago's pilot who is acting as an agent for the Government Reconstruction Committee, which was established among the upper class of the former United Nations Earth.
He is the older brother of Olba Frost.
Although he was listed as a Newtype candidate, he was not compatible with the Flash System and branded as a Category F, the F standing for Fake, and rejected.
To get revenge on a world which refused to acknowledge his unique abilities, he swore to destroy everything.
He is a calm strategist and has no mercy for anyone who gets in his way. He has a unique telepathic ability which allows him to communicate with his brother regardless of distance.

He is the younger brother of Shagia Frost.
Although he was listed as a Newtype candidate, he was not compatible with the Flash System and branded as a Category F, the F standing for Fake, and rejected.
To get revenge on a world which refused to acknowledge his unique abilities, he swore to destroy everything.
He has a cool demeanor and great pride, and is easily agitated compared to Shagia. He has a unique telepathic ability which allows him to communicate with his brother regardless of distance.
