The mayor of Fort Seven.
Once known as Professor Dorat, a commissioned engineer officer for the Space Revolutionary Army.
He descended to Earth during Operation Lilac at the end of the 7th Space War, assuming a new identity after it failed.
However, he never abandoned the mission, attempting to see it through to the end.
And so, under the pretext of defending the city, he conducted research into Cyber Newtypes and restored the mobile armor Patulia.

The head of the Alternative Company.
He was once a technical officer in the United Nation Earth forces, and he took over a former UNE mobile suit testing facility after the war and founded the Alternative Company.
The company grew in influence by developing mobile suits and related equipment.

An executive for the Alternative Company.
He was the head of the company laboratory, but evidentially, was primarily in charge of special operations.

A freelance mobile suit pilot.
She obtained information on the Gundam X from an information broker and attacked it in an attempt to seize it for her own.
However, the Gundam incapacitated her mobile suit in the ensuing fight, and a rival pilot crushed her cockpit, killing her.

A Vulture captain.
He commanded the amphibious battleship Trieste, and he reportedly had a close relationship with the mobile suit pilot Ennil El.
In his greed, he raided power plants and other facilities his fellow Vultures would not dare to.

A second lieutenant in the New United Nations Earth who earned the nickname the White Devil on the African front.
He had a strange obsession with the color white, and his mobile suits were always painted white.
He piloted the mobile suit Correl.

A second lieutenant in the New United Nations Earth who earned the nickname the Shadow on the Eastern front.
He had a split personality-one, a kind-hearted young man, and the other, a bloodthirsty brute.
He piloted the mobile suit Britova.

A second lieutenant in the New United Nations Earth who fought on the Indian front.
A feared ace pilot, he was also known as the Killing Machine.
He challenged the Freeden in the Gable.

A lieutenant in the New United Nations Earth who was sent to the Frost brothers from the Pacific front.
He possessed Newtype abilities and activated the Flash System in the Rasveht, but he was murdered by the Frost brothers.

A member of Satyricon, an anti-government group.
A lively, headstrong girl, fighting for her home.

A member of Satyricon, an anti-government group.
A lively, headstrong girl, fighting for her home.

Captain of the Space Revolutionary Army.
An exceptional pilot of great skill, serving in the instructional battalion to train new recruits.
During the 7th Space War, he engaged in battle against Jamil's Gundam X multiple times.

Captain of the Space Revolutionary Army.
An exceptional pilot of great skill, serving in the instructional battalion to train new recruits.
During the 7th Space War, he engaged in battle against Jamil's Gundam X multiple times.

A Mobile Suit pilot of the civilian army called Dianna Counter, preparing for the Resettlement Operation organized by the Queen of the Moon, Dianna Soreil.
Pilot of the WoDom excavated from the Lunar Mountain Cycle, she descended to Earth with the first return fleet. She was at the frontline during the battle with the Inglessa Militia, fighting the Turn A Gundam several times but with zero victories.
Despite being a skilled Mobile Suit pilot, her fragile mind gives the opponent an upper hand during battles.

A student who lives in the neutral colony Heliopolis.
A slightly reserved and kindhearted young man who specializes in programming. His skill in the field is so advanced that he is capable of freely customizing OS for Mobile Suits.
He used to be an engineering college student, but was caught up in ZAFT's Gundam Capture Operation and unexpectedly wound up piloting the Strike Gundam.
While conflicted over his own nature as a Coordinator, he chose to remain with the Archangel and continue fighting to protect his friends.
He is actually an Ultimate Coordinator, an artificial being born from an artificial womb. He far exceeds other ordinary Coordinators in both his physical abilities and in the bouts of astounding combat prowess he shows when hard-pressed.

An ace pilot of the Earth Alliance Forces, also known as the "Hawk of Endymion."
When ZAFT attacked Heliopolis, the transport vessel he was on got shot down, so he took his Moebius Zero and joined up with the Archangel.
He is an amicable man and a brotherly figure to the Archangel crew, bringing people together despite any personal quarrels or clashes.
He was attracted to Murrue's sense of humanity, and continued working with the Archangel after it left the military.
After the Strike Gundam was repaired at Orb, he took Kira's place piloting it.

An officer in the Earth Alliance Forces and Captain of the Archangel.
She was originally the Archangel's Executive Officer, but the original captain was killed during ZAFT's Gundam Capture Operation, forcing her to take command.
She is a warmhearted woman, and even when a situation calls for rational judgment, she tends to prioritize humaneness over being a proper soldier. This leads to frequent clashes with Natarle Badgiruel, who prioritizes the mission above all else.
She started out rather green and inexperienced, but with some help from veteran soldier and eventual love interest Mu La Flaga, she overcame numerous hardships and grew into a full-fledged Captain in her own right.

An officer in the Earth Alliance Forces and CIC Commander on the Archangel.
She is a skilled officer who handles CIC command while also serving as an executive officer.
She was raised in a military family, and due to her strict adherence to military discipline, she gets into frequent clashes with the more humane Murrue Ramius at very inopportune times. Her rigid attitude makes others see her as coldhearted, but she occasionally shows human signs of weakness, from her low tolerance for alcohol to her clumsiness with children.
After arriving at Alaska Base, she was reassigned to a captainship on board the Dominion. She had her doubts when ordered to subdue the Archangel, but ultimately prioritized her mission and wound up trading cannon fire with her old comrades-in-arms.

A resident of the neutral colony Heliopolis and a friend of Kira Yamato.
He went to engineering college with Kira, but got caught up in the fighting in Heliopolis. He evacuated to the Archangel, and subsequently enlisted in the military and became the ship's assistant helmsman.
He has a friendly and caring demeanor. He and his girlfriend, Miriallia Haw, both support Kira from the sidelines, as Kira garners attention from friends and foes alike as a Coordinator.
Tolle saw the way Kira suffered as the war dragged on, and decided that it was not fair to put all the burden of fighting on Kira alone. 
He trained in a simulator and applied to pilot a Skygrasper.

A commander of the Earth Alliance's 8th Fleet with the rank of Rear Admiral.
He was Murrue Ramius's immediate superior, and he spearheaded the development of mobile suits and G-Weapons within the Alliance.
Both flexible in his thinking and sound in his judgment, even Rau Le Creuset admired his resourceful leadership.

The designated pilot of the Calamity Gundam, a model from the Earth Alliance's newly developed GAT-X series.
He is a Boosted Man, a Natural enhanced via drug therapy and body modifications to outperform a Coordinator. As a Biological CPU, he is treated as just another component of his Mobile Suit, and his past has been completely erased.
During the Alliance's Orb liberation operation, he and compatriots Clotho Buer and Shani Andras were fielded as gamepieces for Muruta Azrael.
Ordinarily a quiet man, his personality shifts to that of a bloodthirsty berserker who makes no distinction between friend and foe in battle.
His main hobby is reading, and he has a particular love for young adult fiction.

The designated pilot of the Raider Gundam, a model from the Earth Alliance's newly developed GAT-X series.
He is a Boosted Man, a Natural enhanced via drug therapy and body modifications to outperform a Coordinator.
As a Biological CPU, he is treated as just another component of his Mobile Suit, and his past has been completely erased.
During the Alliance's Orb liberation operation, he and compatriots Orga Sabnack and Shani Andras were fielded as gamepieces for Muruta Azrael.
Ordinarily a snarky man, his personality shifts to something far more aggressive in battle.
He has destroyed many foes in this state.
His main hobby is video games, and his favorite game genres are action and shooting games.

The designated pilot of the Forbidden Gundam, a model from the Earth Alliance's newly developed GAT-X series.
He is a Boosted Man, a Natural enhanced via drug therapy and body modifications to outperform a Coordinator. As a Biological CPU, he is treated as just another component of his Mobile Suit, and his past has been completely erased.
During the Alliance's Orb liberation operation, he and compatriots Orga Sabnack and Clotho Buer were fielded as gamepieces for Muruta Azrael.
Ordinarily unsociable and deadpan, his personality shifts to take on a much more bare-knuckled combat style in battle that has given Kira Yamato and the rest of the Archangel crew a serious run for their money.
His main hobby is listening to music, and his favorite genres are death metal and industrial music.

A member of ZAFT's Creuset Team and the designated pilot of the Aegis Gundam.
His father is Patrick Zala, a member of the PLANT Supreme Council. His mother died in the "Bloody Valentine" incident.
He carries himself with a resolute demeanor, but is a kind and gentle young man who cares deeply for his friends deep down. He also enjoys tinkering with robotics as a hobby. He has given his fiancee Lacus Clyne many Haros, and gave Birdy to Kira Yamato to symbolize their friendship.
He and Kira have been friends since their days going to elementary school on the Moon together. After their reunion in Heliopolis, they ended up on opposing sides. Athrun bitterly resented having to fight his best friend.
It was when Nicol Amalfi was killed in action that Athrun became determined to take Kira down.

The leader of ZAFT's Creuset Team.
As the man in charge of ZAFT's most elite squadron, he is a calm and capable tactician and also a first-rate Mobile Suit pilot. He wields considerable influence over the PLANT Committee, and has deep ties to Patrick Zala.
He always wears a mask around others and chooses to remain shrouded in mystery. In reality, he is a clone of Al Da Flaga, Mu La Flaga's father. However, he is a defective clone with a shortened telomere that causes him to age rapidly. 
This means he has to stay on a constant drug regimen just to keep himself alive.
He led the Creuset Team in their attack on the neutral colony Heliopolis, and succeeded in capturing the Earth Alliance Force's G Weapons.

The leader of ZAFT's Creuset Team.
As the man in charge of ZAFT's most elite squadron, he is a calm and capable tactician and also a first-rate Mobile Suit pilot. He wields considerable influence over the PLANT Committee, and has deep ties to Patrick Zala.

This means he has to stay on a constant drug regimen just to keep himself alive.
He led the Creuset Team in their attack on the neutral colony Heliopolis, and succeeded in capturing the Earth Alliance Force's G Weapons.

A member of ZAFT's Creuset Team and the designated pilot of the Duel Gundam.
He is a second-gen Coordinator with elite lineage, and a proud man who makes no effort to hide his disdain for Naturals. He has a strong sense of rivalry with his military academy classmate Athrun Zala.
As a member of the Creuset Team, he took part in the attack on the neutral colony Heliopolis and succeeded in capturing the Earth Alliance Force's G Weapons.
After that, Kira Yamato and his Strike Gundam caught Yzak off-guard and left him scarred across his face.
He chose not to remove the scar and vowed revenge, becoming obsessed with hunting down Kira's mothership, the Archangel.

A member of ZAFT's Creuset Team and the designated pilot of the Duel Gundam.
He is a second-gen Coordinator with elite lineage, and a proud man who makes no effort to hide his disdain for Naturals. He has a strong sense of rivalry with his military academy classmate Athrun Zala.
As a member of the Creuset Team, he took part in the attack on the neutral colony Heliopolis and succeeded in capturing the Earth Alliance Force's G Weapons.
After that, Kira Yamato and his Strike Gundam caught Yzak off-guard and left him scarred across his face.
He chose not to remove the scar and vowed revenge, becoming obsessed with hunting down Kira's mothership, the Archangel.

A member of ZAFT's Creuset Team and the designated pilot of the Duel Gundam.
He is a second-gen Coordinator with elite lineage, and a proud man who makes no effort to hide his disdain for Naturals. He has a strong sense of rivalry with his military academy classmate Athrun Zala.
As a member of the Creuset Team, he took part in the attack on the neutral colony Heliopolis and succeeded in capturing the Earth Alliance Force's G Weapons.
After that, Kira Yamato and his Strike Gundam caught Yzak off-guard and left him scarred across his face.
He chose not to remove the scar and vowed revenge, becoming obsessed with hunting down Kira's mothership, the Archangel.

A member of ZAFT's Creuset Team and the designated pilot of the Buster Gundam.
He likes to make quips about other people, and finds common ground with Yzak Joule's belligerence and disdain for Naturals.
As a member of the Creuset Team, he took part in the attack on the neutral colony Heliopolis and succeeded in capturing the Earth Alliance Force's G Weapons.
After that, he continued pursuing the Archangel, but his Mobile Suit was damaged in a fight at Marshall Islands. He was then taken as a POW. 
In meeting Miriallia Haw, he began harboring doubts about the whole point of the war, and eventually chose to side with the Archangel.

A member of ZAFT's Creuset Team and the designated pilot of the Blitz Gundam.
He is a gentle boy who has no heart for fighting, and enlisted in hopes of doing his part to end the war more quickly. 
Chronically unable to harden his heart, Yzak Joule and Dearka Elsman called him a coward. He proved himself otherwise on multiple occasions, such as when he infiltrated and destroyed the Artemis military stronghold as a solo op.
He is close friends with his teammate Athrun Zala, and often intercedes on Athrun's behalf when Athrun gets into arguments with Yzak and Dearka.
As a member of the Creuset Team, he took part in the attack on the neutral colony Heliopolis and succeeded in capturing the Earth Alliance Force's G Weapons.
After that, he continued working with the team to pursue the Archangel.

An ace pilot of ZAFT.
He took part in the Boaz Transport Operation, an op to take a resource satellite called Nova adrift in L4, and bring it to L5 to serve as a ZAFT stronghold. He earned the moniker "Magic Bullet of Dusk" from his performance on the battlefield with his custom-colored orange GINN.
His formidable reputation may have played a part in getting a spot on the Creuset Team, an elite ZAFT force, and taking part in the Gundam Capture Operation. 
However, his custom GINN was destroyed prior to the op in a battle with Gai Murakumo. As a result, he had to pilot a regular GINN for the operation.

Captain of the Vesalius, flagship of the Creuset Team.
He is a calm and collected officer who never loses his cool. 
He serves as Rau Le Creuset's right-hand man.
After taking part in the Gundam Capture Operation, the Creuset Team pursued the Archangel up till the point where the Archangel made planetfall. After that, they received orders to pursue the Eternal. They ended up in a three-way firefight between them, the Archangel, and the Kusanagi at the Mendel colony.

The Commander of ZAFT's African forces.
Known as the "Desert Tiger," he is a capable ground forces Commander and a veteran ace Mobile Suit pilot.
He's generally friendly and down-to-earth, and can be very finicky about his coffee. His paramour Aisha calls him "Andy."
His Waldfeld Team was dispatched to pursue the Archangel after it descended to Earth. He and Aisha challenged Kira Yamato to a duel in their LaGOWE. When Kira awakened, Waldfeld was soundly defeated and went missing. 
He was assumed to be KIA, but survived with grave wounds and eventually made a comeback. 
Patrick Zala held his record in high esteem and made him the captain of the newly-built Eternal.
However, he was part of the peace-promoting Clyne faction.
He bided his time, then stole the Eternal and met up with the Archangel and the Kusanagi, whose crews shared those same ideals.

Executive Officer to Andrew Waldfeld, ZAFT's "Desert Tiger."
He is highly loyal to Waldfeld and supportive of their team. A capable officer, he picks up on his commander's intentions when his CO makes capricious choices, and issues appropriate orders to their subordinates. 
The Archangel had a hard time dealing with this combination of solid leaders.
After Waldfeld fell in battle to Kira Yamato, Dacosta reorganized their remaining forces and left the field.
When they returned, they stumbled across Lowe Guele on the scene. With help from him, they were able to retrieve the badly injured Waldfeld. He then returned to PLANT, where he took up the Clyne faction's banner and worked to support Waldfeld and Lacus Clyne.

A hit idol and pop singer in PLANT.
She is the daughter of Siegel Clyne, Chairman of the PLANT Supreme Council, and also Athrun Zala's fiancee.
She has an innocent and curious disposition that often throws those around her for a loop. She carries a hidden strength deep down, however, her age belies her keen insight.
While en route to Junius Seven, her memorial party came under attack by an Earth Alliance inspection team, and she was forced to escape. She had a fateful encounter with Kira Yamato when he retrieved her lifepod adrift in space.
She returned to PLANT afterward, only to meet Kira again when he was taken there to recover after being badly wounded in a battle with Athrun. They found increasingly common ground together.
When Kira decided to return to the field to get to the bottom of what was driving the war, she entrusted him with the Freedom Gundam and began taking independent action of her own to draw the war to a close.

Daughter of Lord Uzumi Nara Athha of the Orb Union.
A go-getter who believes that actions speak louder than words. She is Kira's sister, but is a Natural herself.
She visited Heliopolis in secret to figure out what her father was up to, which put her in the wrong place at the wrong time when ZAFT attacked. Kira rescued her, and she was returned safely to Orb.
She did not stay home for long, however; she fought against ZAFT as a member of the Desert Dawn resistance movement. There, she was reunited with Kira through sheer coincidence, and forced her way onto the Archangel.
When the Earth Alliance Forces launched the invasion of Orb, Uzumi chose to share the fate of his nation and sent Cagalli away to space aboard the Kusanagi.
Witnessing her father's death was a devastating blow to her, but she was able to move on with some support from Kira and Athrun Zala, and emerged with a new level of emotional maturity.

A test pilot for the M1 Astray, a Mobile Suit developed by Morgenroete, Inc.
A spirited young lady who leads her fellow test pilots, Juri Wu Nien and Mayura Labatt.
When she first piloted the M1 Astray with Kira's newly developed OS, she was astounded at how much its motions had improved.
She took the field for real combat when the Earth Alliance invaded Orb. After the fall of Orb, she joined the Kusanagi and put what little experience her team had to proper use with some solid teamwork.

A test pilot for the M1 Astray, a Mobile Suit developed by Morgenroete, Inc.
A young lady distinguished by her pink glasses.
She made incognito contact with Lowe Guele, and the Red Frame data she gotfrom him contributed greatly to the development of the M1 Astray's os.
She had a bit of a crush on Lowe, but when she saw that Kisato Yamabuki also harbored feelings for him, she chose to distance herself.
She took the field for real combat when the Earth Alliance invaded Orb. After the fall of Orb, she joined the Kusanagi and continued fighting.

A test pilot for the M1 Astray, a Mobile Suit developed by Morgenroete, Inc.
A vibrant, red-haired young lady who struggled along with her teammates to even get the M1 Astray to perform basic functions with its barely working OS.
She took the field for real combat when the Earth Alliance invaded Orb and made it through the battle in spite of her inexperience. After the fall of Orb, she joined the Kusanagi and continued fighting.

An Orb Commissioned Officer.
He was originally a colonel in the 21st Airborne Unit, part of Orb's Ground forces. When Cagalli Yula Athha left Orb, he became a chaperone of sorts that remained a constant presence at her side.
His calm and gentle nature belies his experience serving in the special forces and his expertise in guerrilla warfare. 
When he and Cagalli joined the Desert Dawn resistance, he fought bravely against ZAFT Mobile Suits.
When the Earth Alliance invaded Orb, he took command of the military as Cagalli's aide until the time directly before the fall of Orb, when Uzumi Nara Athha ordered that he and Cagalli take the Kusanagi and escape into space. 
He served as captain of the Kusanagi from that point onward.

A ZAFT pilot and member of the Joule Team.
She is one of a small handful of female Mobile Suit pilots. 
Before enlisting in the military, she was involved in research for a directed energy oscillation system. 
She later became the test pilot of the CGUE DEEP Arms, a suit developed as a Beam Weapon prototype.
When that suit was finished, she was dispatched to the frontlines where she piloted it and took part in a battle in the Jachin Due sector.
She also once fought against the Red Frame when she sortied to retrieve classified data that had fallen into Lowe Guele's hands during the development of the prototype Beam Weapon.

AKA "Ed the Ripper," an Earth Alliance Force pilot.
He was originally a fighter pilot for the United States of South America, until the USSA's integration by force into the Earth Alliance. 
He then became a member of the Earth Alliance military.
He achieved only middling results as a fighter pilot, but he began to shine once he started piloting Mobile Suits. He is especially gifted in hand-to-hand suit combat; his nickname of "Ed the Ripper" comes from the time his suit came back from the frontlines splattered in the oil of enemy suits he had slashed through, making it look bloodstained.
Despite his name, he has no true thirst for battle. Outside of his suit, he is a friendly jokester popular with the soldiers.

AKA the "Mad Dog in the Moonlight," an Earth Alliance Force pilot.
He used to belong to a Eurasian tank squadron.
He got dubbed the "Mad Dog in the Moonlight" due to his specialization in nighttime combat operations.
The "Mad Dog" part comes from the fact that he proposes plans that seem crazy at a glance, but he is actually a shrewd tactician.
He was defeated soundly in the African front by a Mobile Suit team led by Andrew Waldfeld, AKA the "Desert Tiger."
That incident profoundly impressed upon Morgan the superiority of Mobile Suits, which eventually led to him establishing the strategic basis of the Earth Alliance's Mobile Suit usage.
He performed well as a test pilot for the Strike Dagger, and he piloted a 105 Dagger on the field.
When it came to light that he was capable of controlling a Gunbarrel, he took on a Gunbarrel Striker intended for Mu La Flaga, and put it to great use in the Battle of Boaz.

AKA the "Doctor," a ZAFT pilot.
He used to work as a physician, but he enlisted in the military when hostilities broke out.
Indifferent, untrusting, and pessimistic by nature, his highest priority is always his own interests.
Even his prior medical career was solely to satisfy his curiosity about the human body.
As a doctor, he always had calm judgment and a steady hand for removing lesions in operations.
It was no different for him after trading in his scalpel for a Mobile Suit controller; his spot judgment and ability to act swiftly carried over in full from one career to the next.
He likes to characterize combat ops as "Medical Operations," and his surgical precision in identifying and removing the lynchpin of the enemy force that he sees as an afflicted area has earned him the nickname "Doctor" with friends and foes alike.
He has had numerous engagements with the Serpent Tail mercenary group, and Gai Murakumo in particular has given him a considerably hard time.

A youth belonging to the Junk Guild.
He makes a living with his friends in the Junk Guild by restoring Mobile Suits using scrapped suits and parts.
His preternatural ability to identify the strengths and faults of a piece of tech at a glance makes his position within a Guild a point of serious pride. 
Lowe is passionate above all, despite being rough around the edges with a tendency to shoot first and ask questions later.
On a mission to collect junk parts, he paid a visit to Heliopolis shortly after its destruction in the war between the Alliance and the ZAFT where he stumbled upon the Orb's secret prototype Gundam Astrays and took them for the Guild. Now finding himself drawn into a world of trouble, his natural backbone and ingenuity, along with help from his friends in the Junk Guild, helped him to overcome many tough situations.

A member of Lowe Guele's Junk Guild.
Though a genetically-modified Coordinator, he is the twin to a Natural brother. Perhaps due to his birth, he makes a habit of observing Natural behavior, especially Lowe's.
The calm in a storm of quirks, he prefers to maintain a low profile with the whys and wherefores of what he does, like fighting in a maintenance GINN when his friends are in trouble, known only to himself.

Leader of the Serpent Tail mercenaries.
The Serpent Tail, a group with a reputation for taking only the best of the best, is led by this brilliant mercenary whose keen combat sense leaves him unflappable no matter the circumstances.
Though he started as an Alliance-created Combat Coordinator, he struck out on his own once he escaped their laboratory. 
Though his innate combat abilities are a gift of genetic conditioning, he owes a great deal of his skill to his adventures as a mercenary.
Contracted to dispose of both the Orb's secret Gundam Astrays and any unlucky witnesses, he headed to the colony Heliopolis only to happen upon the Junk Guild's Lowe Guele in the act of taking them. His employer's attempt to tie up loose ends found him fighting alongside Lowe to escape the danger. 
given the Astray Blue Frame by Lowe in way of apology, he used the versatile machine to take contracts that could be best described as impossible.

Member of the Serpent Tail mercenaries.
A former ZAFT soldier who found himself a member of the Serpent Tail after a stint as a free agent.
Strikingly beautiful but physically indistinguishable from a Natural despite being a Coordinator, he has come to think of himself as the picture of fashion over form.
Though his body is riddled with scars from his endless brushes with death as a fresh mercenary, he is now more than capable of standing toe-to-toe with any of his comrades.

An heir of the Sahaku family, one of the five ruling families of Orb.
He is a Coordinator and the twin brother of Rondo Mina Sahaku.
Although he is in charge of Mobile Suit development with Orb, he is also a top-notch pilot and heads to the battlefield in the Gundam Astray Gold Frame.
He opposed the neutralism of the Orb representative Uzumi Nara Athha. After the invasion of Orb by the Earth Alliance Forces, he strived to rebuild his homeland in hopes of achieving "global governance by the chosen," an ideal he long cherised.

A woman introduced by the Junk Guild's union and who showed up in front of Lowe Guele and the others to offer support on maintenance work.
Marlin is just an alias, and she's actually Juri Wu Nien, an M1 Astray test pilot with Orb.
To perfect the flawed M1 Astray OS, she conceals her identity and gets in touch with Lowe, who is highly skilled at piloting the Astray series.
She stole the Gundam Astray Red Frame the moment she found an opening, but was unfortunately attacked by a mercenary GINN pilot hired by Actaeon Industries.
Luckily, Lowe saves her. She returns to the Orb Union with the Red Frame's OS data after that.

An heir of the Sahaku family, one of the five ruling families of Orb.
She is a Coordinator and the twin sister of Rondo Gina Sahaku.
She openly criticizes the neutralism of the Orb representative Uzumi Nara Athha, and does not believe her country can be protected by naive thinking. 
Thus,she secretly handles Mobile Suit development for the Earth Alliance Forces and reuses the technology to develop the Astray series. 
After Orb's destruction, she used the space station Ame-no-Mihashira as her base and waited for an opportunity to restore her country to power.
Although her ideals were initially the same as those of her brother Rondo Gina Sahaku, and her plans revolved around world domination, she changed her thinking after her brother's death, meeting Lowe Guele, and other events.

An old man who lives on the abandoned residential satellite the Graveyard.
Although advanced in years, this master swordsman possesses astounding physical abilities, and he even manages to cut off a Mobile Suit's arm without the aid of a suit. In addition, he possesses suit piloting skills even though he's a Natural.
He spent the later years of his life as the Graveyard's caretaker, where he fought against invading looters who were searching for the technologies left there.
When Lowe Guele showed up at the Graveyard in search of a sword to replace his Beam Saber, the old man was at first suspicious and attacked, but gradually came to recognize Lowe's spirit.
After that, he taught Lowe his sword skills and swordsmithing techniques, and even helped repair the Gerbera Straight, a fine blade that was almost completely rusted away.

A young man ordered by Reverend Malchio to retrieve the Dreadnought Gundam.
He is referred to by the Reverend as "the Child of Destiny," and is actually a clone created by the Earth Alliance Forces.
The Alliance Forces only had a few people capable of handling special weapons such as Gunbarrels, and he was born as a result of their efforts to resolve the issue by creating clones with the desired ablities.
However, there were problems with his cloning process, and his life span is extremely short.
He refuses to live his life as a weapon, and confronts his situation with both kindness and courage.
He also strives to reach an understanding with Canard Pars, who faces the same sad fate as him, in spite of their status as enemies.

A pilot with the Eurasian Federation of the Earth Alliance.
He is a member of the Special Forces Team X, which tests Mobile Suits independently developed by the Eurasian Federation, and pilots the Hyperion Gundam.
He boasts extremely advanced piloting skills and is a combat specialist proficient in organized combat as well.
During combat, he is sometimes highly emotional and aggressive, showing no mercy to his enemies.
He is two-faced in a sense; composed at some times and off the rails at others.
Canard is a living example of one of the failed attempts to create a Super Coordinator on Mendel, and harbors strong hatred for Kira Yamato, the only successful example of such a Coordinator.
After losing to Prayer Reverie's Dreadnought Gundam during an operation, Carnard became obsessed with fighting him as well.

A pilot with the Eurasian Federation.
The pet pilot of Rear Admiral Gerard Garcia, who serves on the space fortress Artemis.
He pilots the Hyperion Gundam Unit 2.
Although he calls himself the "Eagle of Artemis," he does not have any real combat experience and is nowhere near Canard Pars, the other Hyperion pilot, in terms of skill.
His Mobile Suit has seven kill marks on it, but all of them were actually scored in simulations.
When Canard escaped from the Artemis, he deployed in the Hyperion in pursuit under Garcia's orders.

The leader of the Zala faction remnants who aim to prevent peace between PLANT and the Earth Alliance, and plot to reignite the war.
The loss of her husband and child in the Bloody Valentine tragedy marked the beginning of her descent into madness.
Volunteering herself for the front lines, she saw many operations through to success alongside her second-in-command, Allen. Afterwards, she was assigned to Patrick Zala's personal guard and became commander of the Jachin Due's First Defense Fleet.
Following the destruction of Jachin Due, and after hearing the news of Patrick's death, she planned to commit suicide, but was stopped by Allen.
Wanting to avenge Patrick and revive the Zala faction, she makes the difficult decision to break away from ZAFT and go into hiding.

Second-in-command to Shiva, who plots to prevent peace between PLANT and the Earth Alliance.
He first met Siva via the Zodiac Alliance, and they have worked together at multiple points throughout the course of their political activities.
He often plays a supporting role to Siva, and he eventually became her second-in-command when she was promoted to commander after the founding of ZAFT.
The death of his parents at the hands of Naturals is a factor in his strong support of Patrick Zala and his eponymous faction.
Following the Second Battle of Jachin Due, he persuaded Siva to break away from ZAFT. 
He continues to rally Zala faction soldiers to their cause while waiting for the right moment to return.

A pilot for ZAFT's new warship, the Minerva.
He used to be a civilian living in the neutral nation of Orb, but his whole family got caught up in the fighting when the Earth Alliance invaded Orb. He then moved to PLANT, where he attended ZAFT military academy and graduated with outstanding grades. 
He was selected to pilot the Impulse Gundam and assigned to the newly- built warship Minerva.
He has a special hatred for Orb for professing to be neutral while allowing the Alliance to come right in and invade, and for failing to protect his family.
He also loathes war itself, and he is easily swayed by emotions in battle.
Armory One, the military arsenal where the Minerva was stationed, came under attack by an Alliance special forces unit called the Phantom Pain. 
This led to Shinn getting a baptism of fire for his first real combat.
As part of the Minerva crew, he kept going through battle after harsh battle, which helped him to improve and grow into an expert fighter.

Pilot of the Freedom Gundam, reputed to be the most powerful Mobile Suit, in the last great war.
Though he remains kindhearted, the toll of so many battles has turned him into a man of forceful conviction.
Living quietly with Lacus Clyne after the war, his life took a turn after she was saved from the jaws of death.
He vowed to stand against the coming chaos and find the road to peace along with his friends on the Archangel.
Though his Freedom Gundam was shot down by Shinn Asuka's Impulse Gundam during the Alliance's Operation Angel down, he returned to the battlefield after receiving the Strike Freedom Gundam from Lacus. 
Gilbert Dullindal's subsequent revelation of the "Destiny Plan" saw him join the decisive battle in space to prevent it from coming to fruition.

Captain of the Archangel.

After the last war, she worked for Orb's Morgenroete,
Inc. under the name Maria Bernes.

After the attempted assassination of Lacus Clyne and
the alliance between Orb and the Atlantic Federation,
she left Orb behind to helm the ship again and rescue
Cagalli Yula Athha from a politically-motivated wedding.
Working with Cagalli, who had steeled herself to save
Orb once again, she joined Orb forces in the fight against
Gilbert Durandal as he tried to push through his
"Destiny Plan."

Showing a maturity in the battle far beyond what she had
in the last war, her cool, clearheaded command was enough
to give the Minerva a run for its money.

A pilot for ZAFT's new warship, the Minerva.
She was in the same class as Shinn Asuka and Rey Za Burrel at the ZAFT military academy, and is similarly allowed to wear a red uniform, the symbol of the top military elites. She has a younger sister named Meyrin Hawke who serves as the Minerva's control officer.
She has nerves of steel, and her plucky and amicable demeanor makes her popular with boys and girls alike.
She is caring and considerate of her friends as well.
As the Minerva's battles get more brutal, and Shinn starts to become more brooding, she makes it a point to reach out to him.
Even in the pitched battle that ensued when the Phantom Pain launched a surprise attack on Armory One, she kept a firm mindset and treated it as a personal challenge, enabling her to hold her own.
She developed a crush on Athrun Zala when he got stationed on the Minerva. However, Athrun and Meyrin deserted ZAFT together. To make matters worse, Shinn shot down their vessel. She was devastated by the loss of her sister, and Shinn was likewise distressed at
having killed them both. The two grew closer by taking comfort in one another.

A pilot for ZAFT's new warship, the Minerva.
He was in the same class as Shinn Asuka and Lunamaria Hawke at the ZAFT military academy, and is similarly allowed to wear a red uniform, the symbol of the top military elites.
He is a preeminent pilot who always maintains a cool head and judges combat situations objectively, and serves as leader for the Minerva's Mobile Suit team.
He is a reserved man about whom little is known.
In reality, he is a clone of Rau Le Creuset, the mastermind behind the previous war. Much like Rau, he is doomed to have a short lifespan.
Gilbert Dullindal has treated him kindly since he was a boy.
He reveres Dullindal like a father and devotes what little life he has to realizing Dullindal's ambitions.

Commander of ZAFT's Joule Team.
He left ZAFT briefly in the previous war, but Gilbert Dullindal arranged for him to return to the fold as Commander of the Joule Team and promoted him to a white uniform.
He still has his old chip on his shoulder, but it is now tempered by a commander's discipline and calm.
He led his team to destroy Junius Seven during "Break the World," and continued fighting Earth Alliance forces in defense of PLANT.

A FAITH pilot assigned to the Minerva.
He was on the Hawkins Team at the Battle of Jachin Due, where he made a name for himself as a ZAFT ace.
He was appointed FAITH in light of his battlefield achievements.
When hostilities with the Earth Alliance resumed, he was right there on the frontlines in his orange-painted ZAKU Phantom, and was responsible for downing a considerable number of Alliance warships.
Later, Gilbert Dullindal dispatched him to the Minerva, where his liberal personality set him apart from their Mobile Suit team leader Athrun Zala.
He was an inspiration to all of the Minerva pilots.

A Lacus Clyne impersonator.
When Lacus vanished from center stage after the last war, she showed up to take Lacus' place in the public eye.
She used to be a regular girl and aspiring singer.
She had a voice very close to that of Lacus, which led to Gilbert Dullindal offering her a position as a Lacus impostor.
She had plastic surgery to give her Lacus' face, and even had Lacus' former fiancé Athrun Zala fooled for a moment.
She took an interest in Athrun and often used her impostor status as an excuse to approach him.
She won the hearts of PLANT citizens with mass appeal even greater than the real Lacus, but started to have doubts about Dullindal.

Commander of the Alliance's Phantom Pain special forces unit.
Ever enigmatic, he uses a mask to cover half of his face.
Though his mannerisms does not fit the bill of a leader, his excellent soldiering and command of the battlefield
allows him to lead the Minerva around by the nose.
His rare ability to operate the Exass Mobile Armor's multidirectional attack system marks him as an ace pilot.
A masterful manipulator of the notoriously stubborn Extended pilots, it was he who led them in the Gundam heist from the ZAFT's from Armory One.

Neo Roanoke unmasked.
Appearing in the Battle of Berlin as Stella Loussier's escort, he was shot down by Kira Yamato in the Freedom Gundam before being picked up by the Archangel.
It was then that he was revealed to be Mu La Flaga, the man presumed to have been killed in action in the last great war. 
He had been recovered by the Alliance after the Battle of Jachin Due and implanted with the alter ego Neo Roanoke, his peerless skill twisted to their purposes.
Though his rescue did not help to restore his memories, the undeniable echoes of his past with ex-lover Murrue Ramius and the ship led him to make the choice to fight alongside her and the crew.

A pilot for the Earth Alliance special forces unit Phantom Pain.
He is an Extended, one of the Earth Alliance's artificially enhanced soldiers.
He can be belligerent, but he has solid reasoning and calm judgment.
He acts as the leader of his fellow Extended, Auel Neider and Stella Loussier.
He infiltrated the military arsenal Armory One together with his teammates. He stole the Chaos Gundam, which he went on to pilot in repeated engagements with the ZAFT warship Minerva.
He later had his memories manipulated each time he lost a teammate, and this treatment left him emotionally unstable. 
As a Biological CPU for controlling a Mobile Suit, he continued fighting solely out of a lust for battle.
The Extended are conditioned to have "block words" that can drive them into a panic in order to get their outbursts under control, but it is unclear what his words are.

A pilot for the Earth Alliance special forces unit Phantom Pain.
She is an Extended, one of the Earth Alliance's artificially enhanced soldiers.
She often struggles with words and acts fairly docile, but demonstrates ferocious capabilities when piloting a Mobile Suit.
She infiltrated the military arsenal Armory One together with her teammates, Sting Oakley and Auel Neider. 
She stole the Gaia Gundam, which she went on to pilot in repeated engagements with the ZAFT warship Minerva.
While on shore leave in Diocuia, she accidentally slipped and fell into the water. Shinn Asuka rescued her, and they grew increasingly intimate with one another.
The Extended are conditioned to have "block words" that can drive them into a panic in order to get their outbursts under control. Stella's block word is "Die."

A pilot for the Earth Alliance special forces unit Phantom Pain.
He is an Extended, one of the Earth Alliance's artificially enhanced soldiers.
He has a freewheeling personality with a competitive streak.
He has intense emotional highs and lows, and is prone to frequent outbursts.
He infiltrated the military arsenal Armory One together with his teammates, Sting Oakley and Stella Loussier.
He stole the Abyss Gundam, which he went on to pilot in repeated engagements with the ZAFT warship Minerva.
The Extended are conditioned to have "block words" that can drive them into a panic in order to get their outbursts under control. Auel's block word is "Mother."

A pilot of FAITH, assigned to the Minerva.
Working as Cagalli Yula Athha's guard after the war, Gilbert Dullindal demanded his return to ZAFT.
He then became a member of the FAITH special forces group, taking a post on the Minerva with his Saviour Gundam.
While he worked to lead a squadron of the Minerva's Mobile Suits, he made his escape from the ship after harboring doubts about Dullindal's much-touted justice.
Critically injured after being shot down by Shinn Asuka during the course of his escape, he was picked up by the Archangel and reunited with Kira Yamato.
With the Infinite Justice Gundam brought by Lacus Clyne standing before him, he quashed his doubts about fighting and vowed to join Kira and the crew in defeating Dullindal.

Bodyguard of Orb Chief Representative Cagalli Yula Athha.
Alex Dino was the alias used by Athrun Zala when he defected to Orb after the last great war.
Escorting Cagalli at Armory One when the Gundam heist occurred, he expended every effort to protect her in
his ZAKU Warrior. A chain of events led him to a post on the ZAFT's new Minerva, drawing him into "Operation Break the World," the spark that would reignite the flames of war.

A former ZAFT Commander, once known as the "Desert Tiger."
Waldfeld spent many peaceful days with Kira Yamato and the others following the end of the last war.
After the attempt on Lacus Clyne's life and the alliance between Orb and the Atlantic Federation, he started up the Archangel once more.
From there, he left for space with Lacus, who was being imitated by Meer Campbell.
They returned to the Eternal, hidden away since the end of the war, where he resumed duties as Captain.
He has a prosthetic arm and leg, and his left arm conceals a gun.

The sole female member of a trio of Dom Trooper pilots who belong to the Clyne faction.
With her at the center, the team skillfully maneuvers their heavyweight Doms to unleash triple-point attacks.

An indispensable member of the Junk Guild.
As a mechanical wizard, he can spot points of failure at a glance.
He takes great pride in his work as a junk dealer.
Although he is a Natural, he can pilot the Red Frame with the support of his partner, 8.

One of Celestial Being's Gundam Meisters.
A young man who fights to eliminate war, Setsuna is the Meister for Gundam Exia, which resides on the Ptolemaios. He calls himself "Gundam."
Born and raised in the Middle Eastern Republic of Krugis, Setsuna became embroiled in war at a young age and fought as a child soldier. He was saved by the 0 Gundam, which led to him eventually becoming one of Celestial Being's Gundam Meisters.
Relatively cold and calculating for his age, he tends to dislike interacting with others. His encounter with Marina Ismail, Princess of Azadistan, and his experiences fighting alongside his fellow Gundam Meisters inspired considerable character growth.
The name Setsuna F. Seiei is a codename within Celestial Being. His real name is Soran Ibrahim.

One of Celestial Being's Gundam Meisters.
An expert marksman capable of sniping an orbital target from the ground, Lockon is the Meister of Gundam Dynames, which resides aboard the Ptolemaios.
Terrorists killed his parents 10 years ago, causing him to have a strong hatred of terrorism. This eventually led to him becoming a member of Celestial Being, which seeks to eliminate conflict.
A cheerful and caring young man, he frequently helps support the other members of Celestial Being as something of a big brother figure, particularly the oft-troubled Setsuna F. Seiei.
Lockon Stratos is just his codename within Celestial Being; his real name is Neil Dylandy.

One of Celestial Being's Gundam Meisters.
The Meister for Gundam Kyrios, which resides aboard the Ptolemaios.
Allelujah underwent physical modifications and mental conditioning at the Human Reform League's Super Human Research Institute that made him stronger than the average human, and later fled the facility to join Celestial Being.
Though normally a warm and caring young man, one of the side effects of his conditioning was the formation of a violent, opposite split personality named Hallelujah Haptism.
He continues participating in armed interventions while struggling against Hallelujah's violent outbursts.
The existence of Soma Peries, an enemy soldier, makes him realize that the Super Human Research Institute is still in operation. This led him to propose an operation to destroy the Institute and end the vicious cycle.

An alternate personality lurking within Allelujah
Haptism, one of Celestial Being's Gundam Meisters.
Hallelujah is an alternate personality that was born from the physical modifications and mental conditioning Allelujah underwent at the Human Reform League's Super Human Research Institute.
Violent and eager to fight, he is the polar opposite of Allelujah, and does not hesitate to kill. Hallelujah tends to surface when Allelujah is faced with a crisis, and will act in ways that Allelujah would be opposed to (such as murdering others).
In the final battle against the UN Forces, Allelujah and Hallelujah fought together against Sergei Smirnov and Soma Peries. Allelujah, handling thought, and Hallelujah, handling reflexes, worked together as originally intended to unleash the powers of a true Super Soldier.

One of Celestial Being's Gundam Meisters.
The Meister for Gundam Virtue, which resides aboard the Ptolemaios.
Tieria has access rights to Veda, the core computer system behind Celestial Being, and is a particularly mysterious member of the organization.
He has absolute confidence in his abilities and demands perfection from others. He is even willing to eliminate a fellow Gundam Meister if he deems them unsuitable.
Tieria views Veda's plan as absolute and acts accordingly.
He is exasperated by the other Meisters, whose unexpected behavior results in frequent crises that occasionally force the scrapping of entire missions.

Celestial Being's tactical forecaster.

Effectively the commander of the Ptolemaios, Celestial
Being's tactical mothership, Sumeragi issues mission
plans and orders to the Gundam Meisters.

Though she once worked as a tactical forecaster for
the AEU, a tactical error resulted in the death of her
beloved, and led to her joining Celestial Being to aid
their efforts to eliminate conflict.

Though she vowed to never make another mistake, past
trauma leads her to drink prodigiously in an effort to
conceal weakness, even during missions.

Sumeragi Lee Noriega is her codename within Celestial
Being. Her real name is Lisa Kujo.

Celestial Being's gunner.
A reserve Gundam Meister, he can usually be found serving as support for Lichtendahl Tsery on the Ptolemaios.
Lasse is a young man with a powerful, well-built physique.
Though he often expresses himself in a cynical manner, he takes his mission seriously and is sincerely trusted by the rest of the crew.
When the GN Arms was provided for combat, he became its pilot and demonstrated his considerable gunnery skills to assist Setsuna F. Seiei and the rest of the Gundam Meisters in combat.

One of Celestial Being's Gundam Meisters.
A member of Team Trinity, Johann is the Meister for Gundam Throne Eins.
Created via genetic manipulation for the express purpose of becoming a Gundam Meister, Johann is the eldest brother of the Trinity Siblings.
He is also the most calm and collected of the three, executing missions faithfully while keeping his less well-behaved brother and sister in check.
As a new Gundam Meister, he participated in armed interventions separate from those of the Ptolemaios, but Team Trinity's use of excessive force and violence only served to draw criticism, inflaming anti-Gundam sentiments around the world.

One of Celestial Being's Gundam Meisters.
A member of Team Trinity, Michael is the Meister for Gundam Throne Zwei. 
Created via genetic manipulation for the express purpose of becoming a Gundam Meister, Michael is the youngest brother of the Trinity Siblings.
He is short-tempered, prone to violence, and rude. He also dotes on his younger sister Nena, and views any man who dares to approach her as a bitter enemy.
As a new Gundam Meister, he participated in armed interventions separate from those of the Ptolemaios, but Team Trinity's use of excessive force and violence only served to draw criticism, inflaming anti-Gundam sentiments around the world.

A Gundam Meister of Celestial Being.
She is a member of Trinity, Celestial Being's second team, and she pilots the Gundam Throne Drei.
Created via genetic engineering for the express purpose of becoming a Gundam Meister, Nena is the youngest of the Trinity siblings. Although she has a capricious and artless personality, she also has a sadistic side and cares little for the lives of others.
As a new Gundam Meister, she participated in armed interventions separate from those of the Ptolemaios, but Team Trinity's use of excessive force and violence only served to draw criticism, inflaming anti-Gundam sentiments around the world.
Note that Nena is also the only Trinity member who can access Veda, though her ability is limited.

An Observer of Celestial Being.
He is the current head of the Corner family, whose members have long acted as the Observers of Celestial Being.
Although he is officially a United Nations ambassador of the nations that belong to the Union, he secretly acts as a Celestial Being Observer even as he schemes to take over Aeolia Schenberg's plan, a long-held ambition of the Corner family.
He gradually twisted Aeolia Schenberg's plan, such as by providing independently developed Gundam Thrones to Trinity to conduct armed interventions separate from the Ptolemaios and by giving GN-X Mobile Suits with onboard solar reactors to various nations when the United Nations Forces were established.
During the final battle with Celestial Being, Alejandro sortied in the gigantic Mobile Armor Alvatore and clashed with the Gundam Exia in an effort to destroy the organization with his own hands.

An ace pilot of the Union.
Graham is a Flag Fighter affiliated with MSWAD, and his outstanding skills as a Mobile Suit pilot are evident in achievements like pulling off a mid-air transformation (dubbed the "Graham Special") in his Union Flag, which is a maneuver the suit was not even designed for.
Although Graham is loyal to his mission, a proud soldier, and serious to a fault, he also has a passionate side, as shown by his obsession with Celestial Being's Gundams.
After first seeing Celestial Being's Gundam Exia at the AEU's military training grounds and later squaring off with the Exia, he ended up getting transferred to the Anti-Gundam Investigative Squad. He was later appointed leader of the Overflags (the 8th Independent Tactical Fighter Squadron) and continued his dogged pursuit of Gundams.

A Moible Suit pilot of the Union.

Howard is a Flag Fighter assigned to the Anti-Gundam
Investigative Squad at Graham Aker's request.

Although generally calm and collected, he is prouder
than anyone to be a Flag Fighter, and even scolds his
colleague Daryl Dodge for lamenting the difference in
performance between the Union Flag and Gundams.

After being assigned to the Anti-Gundam Investigative
Squad, he joined the Overflags (the 8th Independent
Tactical Fighter Squadron) and participated in many
operations as Graham's subordinate.

A Mobile Suit pilot of the Union.

Like Howard Mason, Daryl is a Flag Fighter assigned to
the Anti-Gundam Investigative Squad.

He reveres squad leader Graham Aker, and it infuriated
him when Graham was disrespected by Joshua Edwards and
Patrick Colasour.

After being assigned to the Anti-Gundam Investigative

An ace pilot of the AEU.
Although Patrick has never suffered a single defeat in 2,000 mock battles and is unquestionably skilled as a pilot, his inflated sense of self and belligerence are viewed as problems by the top brass.
He also falls in love at first sight with Kati Mannequin, his commanding officer, and takes advantage of every opportunity to try to win her affections.
Patrick was selected from among many to be the test pilot for the AEU Enact, a cutting-edge Mobile Suit. However, Celestial Being's Gundam Exia showed up during the training exercises and destroyed the AEU Enact as part of the organization's first armed intervention. Patrick thus suffered the disgrace of becoming the first pilot in the world to lose to a Gundam. To redeem himself, he went on to fight Gundams countless times. Although he always lost, he always made it home in one piece, and people soon started calling him "the Immortal Colasour."

A Mobile Suit team operational commander of the AEU.
Even as a student, Kati was well-known as a tactical forecaster. She was recruited by the AEU, and the Celestial Being member Sumeragi Lee Noriega was one of her contemporaries in college.
Kati has a sharp mind and a collected personality. As a soldier, she is also extremely strict, and accidentally wins the affections of Patrick Colasour when she punches him for being late to a strategy meeting.
She participated in the joint military exercises conducted by the three major powers as the AEU's operational commander, was entrusted with duties including command of the GN-X team after the United Nations Forces were formed, and dedicated herself to hunting down Celestial Being.

A Mobile Suit team commander of the Human Reform League.
Sergei is a seasoned soldier also known as the "Wild Bear of Russia." An experienced suit pilot, he has stood on the frontlines of many engagements, and is an exceptional commander with countless military achievements to his name. He is also trusted by his subordinates.
Sergei was similarly trusted by his commanding officer, Commander Kim, and was appointed the leader of Chobu, a special forces squad formed to capture a Gundam. He was entrusted with Soma Peries (aka Super Soldier Number 1), a weapon developed by the Super Human Research Institute.
Sergei led teams against Gundams on countless occasions and commanded the GN-X team along with Kati Mannequin after the United Nations Forces were formed.

A pilot of the Human Reform League.
Soma (aka Super Soldier Number 1), is a product of the HRL's Super Human Research Institute, and was developed to be the perfect soldier.
As a Super Soldier, her body and nervous system have been enhanced, her combat capabilities far exceed those of normal Mobile Suit pilots, and she can use Quantum Brainwaves.
Somewhat devoid of emotion, she has little interest in anything other than completing her mission, but her commanding officer Sergei Smirnov treats her like a human being, and she learns to love him as a father.
Soma is assigned to Chobu, the special forces squad led by Sergei, and fights Celestial Being's Gundams on many occasions. She eventually develops a strong sense of rivalry toward Allelujah Haptism due to the interference caused by his Quantum Brainwaves.

A mysterious young man who works with Alejandro Corner.
Although he seems neutral, including a soft demeanor and calm manner, he is an enigmatic figure whose origins are unknown.
While under the protection of the Observer Alejandro Corner, Ribbons obeyed the man even as he closely monitored the actions of Celestial Being's Gundam Meisters.
When Alejandro starts taking action to hijack Aeolia Schenberg's plan, Ribbons controls Veda, which was on the Moon, to help him achieve his vision.
In addition, the young Setsuna F. Seiei, who later became a Gundam Meister, witnesses Ribbons as he pilots the 0 Gundam during the armed intervention in a conflict in the Republic of Krugis.

A mercenary affiliated with PMC Trust.
Al-Saachez is a warmonger who loves to fight and lives only for the thrill of battle.
During his time as the leader of the anti-government guerrilla organization KPSA, he was involved in a conflict in Krugis, where he brainwashed and trained the young Setsuna F. Seiei and others to become child soldiers.
He was also the ringleader behind the terrorist act that resulted in the murder of the Dylandy brothers' family.
In addition to possessing formidable hand-to-hand combat capabilities, he is an exceptional Mobile Suit pilot, and gave the Gundam Exia a lot of trouble when they first battled during the armed intervention in Moralia.
He piloted his Enact Custom, which was inferior in terms of performance.
After that, he contacted the Celestial Being Observer Alejandro Corner, killed two of the Trinity siblings under the Observer's orders, and captured the Gundam Throne Zwei.
He went on to join the United Nations Forces under an assumed name and continued battling Celestial Being's Gundams.

A Celestial Being Gundam Meister.
Although she was an ordinary high school student, her Workloader piloting skills were recognized by Veda, and she was recruited by Grave Violento.
As a Meister, she is involved in the development of the second generation machine Gundam Plutone, and sympathizes with Celestial Being's goal of using armed intervention to eradicate war. However, she struggles to cope with the idea of taking lives in combat to achieve it.
As passionate as her age would suggest, she secretly fell in love with Ruido Resonance, another Gundam Meister.

A Celestial Being Gundam Meister.
The Meister of the Gundam Rasiel, he is an Innovade in charge of recruiting new Gundam Meisters.
He sports sunglasses, long black hair, and has a serious personality. In addition to gathering information, he is highly proficient at both Mobile Suit and hand-to-hand combat.
He contributed to the efforts to recruit talented Gundam Meisters like Neil Dylandy and Allelujah Haptism.

An Innovade who shares the same genetic pattern as Ribbons Almark.
He has the unique ability of copying his own personal data to other Innovades, and is therefore practically immortal.
Although he was once Ribbons' confidante, Beside ended up opposing him due to a difference of opinion regarding Aeolia's plan, and was later killed by Grave Violento.
However, his personality data remained in the 1 Gundam.
He went after Leif Recitativo, hijacked his personality to use his body, and once again started working to realize his ambitions.

An ace pilot with the Earth Federation Peacekeeping Forces.
Formerly with the AEU, he served as a backup pilot on the UN Forces GN-X team. However, he ended up piloting a GN-X after the death of one of the main pilots.
His skills were an extremely good fit for the solar-reactor-powered machine, and the expertise with which he piloted it earned him the nickname the "Steel Cowboy."
He was also called the "Devil of Unification" by the opposing anti-Federation forces, who feared him.
He is loose when it comes to women and quite the playboy.

A boy who lives in the space colony, Nora.
The Asuno family was a family of engineers whose ancestor is the legendary Mobile Suit blacksmith known to have developed
the savior, Gundam. Flit was entrusted with the AGE Device by his mother and cooperated with the Earth Federation Forces to develop Gundam during his academic years.
Flit is an excellent engineer who also demonstrated his talent as a Mobile Suit pilot. During a fierce battle, he awakes the X region, a part of the human brain, awakening him as an X-Rounder that increases prediction ability and reaction speed.
He is a friendly and kind boy, but a perfectionist and selfish side of him makes it hard to get along with people around him. He has a strong hatred for the UE, who took a peaceful life away from him.

A mysterious girl who Flit saved on the Nora colony.

A lieutenant in the Earth Federation Forces known as the White Wolf.
He was once a famous mobile suit racer, and he became a pilot in search of a new challenge.
He is overconfident and vulgar, but his piloting skills are first rate.

The commander of the Arinston Base Security Force's Drace Platoon.

The deputy commander of Arinston base and a lieutenant colonel in the Earth Federation Forces.

A boy Flit met on the Fardain colony.

The commander of the space fortress Ambat.
He confronted the one who welcomed the UE in during "the Day Angel Fell."

A civilian living in the underground slum of Fardain, he settled in between the inner and outer walls of the colony.

The leader of the Zalam, he lived on Fardain.
After the Colony Nation War ended, he was still locked in a power struggle with the Euba.
He even piloted the Gala, a commander mobile suit, and sortied himself.

The leader of the Euba, one of the former nations from the Colony Nation War.
For a long time, he opposed the Zalam, but decided to temporarily join forces with them and the Diva to confront the UE.
He piloted the Elmeda.

The son of the hero, Flit Asuno, and the Gundam pilot whoinherited the AGE Device.

A child soldier in the Third Group affiliated with CGS, a Martian private security firm.
Small and steely-framed with nerves to match, he has endured three highly dangerous Alaya-Vijnana System surgeries.
He does not sweat the details, and appears indifferent most of the time, but is fiercely caring about his friends and downright ruthless to enemies, taking lives on the battlefield with no mercy.
He has a close bond with Orga Itsuka, the friend he has been through thick and thin with since a young age. 
He would do anything for Orga, no matter how dirty the job.
The Alaya-Vijnana System grants him a high degree of spatial awareness, and he has impressive Mobile Suit piloting skills, which led to him piloting Gundam Barbatos when Gjallarhorn attacked CGS.
After forming Tekkadan, he continued serving as Gundam Barbatos' designated pilot for the sake of Orga and his friends.

Leader of the Third Group affiliated with CGS,
a Martian private security firm.

His fine leadership and care for his comrades make him

Group.

He considers his childhood friend Mikazuki Augus closer

In turn, he feels pressured by the hope that Mikazuki
places in him, and strives to meet his expectations.

He used the Gjallarhorn attack as an opportunity to

that had been abusing them, formed them into a group

Later, he earned recognition from Naze Turbine, whom
he met while escorting Kudelia Aina Bernstein to Earth,
and Tekkadan came under Teiwaz's jurisdiction.

He learned what a leader should be from his sworn brother
Naze, giving him newfound resolve to protect Tekkadan,
his family.

A child soldier in the Third Group affiliated with CGS, a Martian private security firm.
He comes from the Dort Colonies, and signed up with CGS to provide for his grandmother and twin sisters.He is a friendly, mild-mannered guy much beloved by his friends.
He has a wide range of knowledge from his days attending school, and often served as an advisor for Orga Itsuka in the Third Group. After the establishment of Tekkadan, he continued serving as an aide to Orga.

A child soldier in the Third Group affiliated with CGS, a Martian private security firm.
He used to be the leader of the Third Group, but Orga usurped that role from him, leaving him at constant odds with Orga.
He has sharp wits and physical prowess to match, but his recklessness made it hard for him to win any trust among his friends. After Tekkadan's establishment, he served as the number-two man after Orga.
He uses the Alaya-Vijnana System to enhance his piloting performance, and Orga has entrusted him with the Isaribi during critical combat junctures.
He used the Alaya-Vijnana System to control two warships simultaneously during a battle in low Earth Orbit, successfully evading the Gjallarhorn Fleet and giving Orga's group the chance to make atmospheric entry.

He called his modified Graze Custom the Ryusei-Go and gave it a bright pink paintjob.

A child soldier in the Third Group affiliated with CGS, a Martian private security firm.
He is the leader of the Human Debris sold to CGS, and has a sturdy body and mind to match. Blunt and unfriendly, he tends to favor keeping to himself and training in silence.
In his younger days, he used to sail the stars with his family in a merchant ship fleet. Unfortunately, his parents were killed in a pirate raid, and he was separated from his younger brother Masahiro and sold as Human Debris.
He became free when Orga Itsuka took over CGS, only to turn around and volunteer to join Tekkadan.
He has undergone Alaya-Vijnana System surgery twice, and is the second-most highly-skilled Mobile Suit pilot in Tekkadan, after Mikazuki Augus.

Once he returned to Mars, he fought well on the frontlines piloting a Landman Rodi.
