A child soldier in the Third Group affiliated with CGS, a Martian private security firm.
Chipper and enthusiastic about his job, he acts as a leader to the younger kids. After Tekkadan's establishment, he was given a job doing gopher work and handling supplies.
He dreams of sending his little sister Fuka to school.
He went out on patrols with Akihiro Altland in hopes of improving his job repertoire, but was badly wounded in an attack by Mobile Suits belonging to the Brewers, a band of space pirates.

A child soldier in the Third Group affiliated with CGS,a Martian private security firm.
He is one of the youngest members of the group.
Though quiet and withdrawn, he has a knack for tinkering, and works as a mechanic providing support for Nadi Yukinojo Kassapa.
After Tekkadan was formed, he performed Mobile Suit and Mobile Worker maintenance, and was solely responsible for Norba Shino's Ryusei-Go.

A Tekkadan child soldier.
He used to be Human Debris and belonged to the Brewers.
After the Brewers were destroyed, he was taken in by Tekkadan and welcomed as a friend along with Aston and the rest of their group.
He piloted a Mobile Worker at the battle of Edmonton and came running to Orga Itsuka's rescue together with Eugene Seven Stark and his group.

A former Human Debris child solder and member of Tekkadan's first operations team.
After the battle of Edmonton, he was assigned to the first operations team when Tekkadan started expanding.
He never had a name back in his Human Debris days, but Akihiro Altland suggested he take on the Altland surname.
He served as the designated pilot for a Landman Rodi taken from the Earth Branch.
He took part in the showdown against the Arianrhod Fleet and made it out alive, minus his left arm.

A Tekkadan child soldier.
He used to be Human Debris and belonged to the Brewers.
After the Brewers were destroyed, he was taken in by Tekkadan, and he cried at Orga Itsuka's words of welcome.
He piloted a Mobile Worker at the battle of Edmonton and came running to Orga Itsuka's rescue together with Eugene Seven Stark and his group.

A key figure in the Mars Independence Movement, she is the
daughter of Norman Bernstein, representative of the Chryse
Autonomous Region.

After her success in gathering pro-independence activists
at the Noachis July Assembly, she won fame as the "Maiden
of Revolution" and became perceived as a threat by
Gjallarhorn.

She hired CGS to escort her to Earth to attend negotiations
with the Arbrau government, and Gjallarhorn attacked her
when they found out. Struck by the cruel lives of the boys
who died in that battle, she became more determined than
ever to head to Earth, and once again hired Tekkadan to be
her escort.

Representative of the Turbines, the company that manages
Teiwaz's transportation division.

He is a dandy who dresses in white business suits, and is
widely known in the Jupiter Sphere as someone unafraid of
resorting to violence. All of the women under his command
are married to him, ostensibly for protection, and he has
had many children by these wives.

He has carried out the ritual exchange of sake cups
representing a father-and-son relationship with McMurdo
Barriston, the representative of Teiwaz, and they have
great faith in one another.

Maruba Arkay got him to start a fight with Tekkadan, but
he was so impressed with the way they fought, and with
Orga Itsuka's determination, that he introduced them to
McMurdo instead.

Afterward, he exchanged sake cups with Orga, making them
sworn brothers who always looked out for one another.

Naze Turbine's first wife.
She first met Naze in her mercenary days, and has been his devoted partner ever since. She is a more-than-capable pilot who uses a Single Number Hyakuren.
When there is a fight, she sorties personally to lead the Mobile Suit team.
She is a strong and spirited woman who is also in touch with her motherly side, and the Turbines members love her like a big sister.

A Mobile Suit pilot with the Turbines.
She is one of Naze Turbine's lovers, and calls him "darling." She has a lively and outgoing personality.
She is also no slouch as a pilot, freely controlling the high-mobility Hyakuri Mobile Suit.
She grew closer to Tekkadan once they came under Teiwaz's umbrella, and started joining Akihiro Altland's group for training.
She accompanied Tekkadan to Earth on Naze's orders and supported them in their battles.

A Mobile Suit pilot with the Turbines.
She is one of Naze Turbine's lovers, and reports to Amida Arca. Her favored Mobile Suit is the Hyakuren.
She is calm and collected, and often works in tandem with fellow pilot Lafter Frankland.
She and Lafter rushed to the rescue of Orga Itsuka's group when they came under attack by Gjallarhorn when descending to Earth. She continued working with Tekkadan and supporting their battles afterward.

Specialist major in Gjallarhorn's auditing division.
He is a young yet sharp-witted man and highly capable Mobile Suit pilot.
He comes from the lowest rung of society, where he stayed until Iznario Fareed, head of the illustrious House Fareed, took him in and adopted him.
He has been close friends with Gaelio Bauduin, a scion of a Seven Stars family, and is engaged to Gaelio's younger sister Almiria.
He frowns upon the corruption within Gjallarhorn. When he visited the Mars branch for an audit, he looked none too kindly upon Coral Conrad, who was exploiting his position as branch CO for personal gain.
He encountered Mikazuki Augus and Tekkadan on Mars, sparking the start of a long relationship.

Specialist major in Gjallarhorn's auditing division.
He is a young yet sharp-witted man and highly capable Mobile Suit pilot.
He comes from the lowest rung of society, where he stayed until Iznario Fareed, head of the illustrious House Fareed, took him in and adopted him.
He has been close friends with Gaelio Bauduin, a scion of a Seven Stars family, and is engaged to Gaelio's younger sister Almiria.
He frowns upon the corruption within Gjallarhorn. When he visited the Mars branch for an audit, he looked none too kindly upon Coral Conrad, who was exploiting his position as branch CO for personal gain.
He encountered Mikazuki Augus and Tekkadan on Mars, sparking the start of a long relationship.

Specialist Major in Gjallarhorn's auditing division.
He is the heir to House Bauduin, one of the Seven Stars.
He is a straightforward and well-bred man with a strong sense of justice.
He is a longtime friend of McGillis Fareed, who serves in the same auditing division, and shares McGillis' desire to reform the corrupt Gjallarhorn.
He fought against Tekkadan while dispatched to the Mars branch as McGillis' escort, but was no match for Mikazuki Augus's Gundam Barbatos. He has pursued Tekkadan aggressively ever since.

Gaelio Bauduin, after taking off his Vidar mask.
His face still bears a large scar from the fight he lost against McGillis Fareed.
He also has an Alaya-Vijnana Type E terminal embedded in his body.
When joined with his Mobile Suit, he is capable of incredible feats of piloting.
When McGillis sought out Gundam Bael in an underground Vingolf facility, Gaelio confronted him and deemed his goal to be attaining raw power.
He revealed himself and decried McGillis' deeds, proclaiming that he would strike McGillis down as a traitor.

Gaelio Bauduin, after taking off his Vidar mask.
His face still bears a large scar from the fight he lost against McGillis Fareed.
He also has an Alaya-Vijnana Type E terminal embedded in his body.
When joined with his Mobile Suit, he is capable of incredible feats of piloting.
When McGillis sought out Gundam Bael in an underground Vingolf facility, Gaelio confronted him and deemed his goal to be attaining raw power.
He revealed himself and decried McGillis' deeds, proclaiming that he would strike McGillis down as a traitor.

An officer in Gjallarhorn's Mars branch.
He is a soldier trained by Crank Zent, and is simple and straightforward. He has a Martian-born mother, and his cohorts use that as an excuse to discriminate against him.
He looked up to Crank for treating him just like any other new soldier, but lost Crank in battle against Tekkadan. He applied to the Tekkadan pursuit team in order to avenge Crank, and pursued Tekkadan under Gaelio Bauduin's leadership.

A veteran officer in Gjallarhorn's Mars branch.
He is an experienced and sensible military man who trains new soldiers.
He treats soldiers from Mars just like everyone else, earning him admiration from Ein Dalton, one of his students.
Coral Conrad ordered him to destroy CGS, but he balked at the idea of battling children and defied orders, choosing to sortie alone.
He challenged Mikazuki Augus and his Gundam Barbatos to a duel with custody of Kudelia Aina Bernstein at stake.

Commander of the Gjallarhorn Outer Earth Orbit Regulatory Joint Fleet.
A young lady of House Issue, the foremost of the Seven Stars, she is a longstanding friend of McGillis Fareed and Gaelio Bauduin. She has had a mild crush on McGillis since they were children.
She is a woman of indomitable will who, while prone to occasional emotional outbursts, carries herself with the bearing of a true noble and earns the trust of her subordinates.
She is well aware that the only reason someone as young as her is in such a position of command is because it is an honorary position given to her by virtue of her birth. She devotes her days to training her fleet in order to prove that she deserves the job.
She is also a skilled Mobile Suit pilot, and goes out personally to command from the front when battles occur.
When Tekkadan reached the Earth Sphere, she led her fleet to intercept them, only to fall for their ploy and let them make planetfall. She pursued Tekkadan doggedly from that point on, in order to regain her lost honor.

Representative of Orcus Company.

He is the person who, through old acquaintance Todo
Mirconen, guided Tekkadan to Earth.

He was secretly in touch with Coral Conrad, Commander of
the Gjallarhorn Mars branch, and leaked intel on Tekkadan
in order to make Coral indebted to him.

When Orga Itsuka's group ascended to space, he personally
led a warship alongside Gjallarhorn and launched an attack
on them.

Leader of the Brewers, a band of space pirates notorious
for their violent ways.

He leads a pirate group that preys on Martian transport
routes and makes its money looting merchant ships.

McGillis Fareed picked him up and hired him to abduct
Kudelia Aina Bernstein, leading to his attack against
Tekkadan.

Once he had Gjallarhorn's backing, he grew cocky and
picked fights with the Turbines, a Teiwaz subsidiary
organization.

A Mobile Suit pilot for the Brewers, a band of space pirates.
He is the number two man in the Brewers, where he commands the Mobile Suit team and pilots the Gundam Gusion.
He has a quirky personal aesthetic, and occasionally switches to an effeminate way of talking.
He is a solid pilot, but rather unpopular due to the way he treats Human Debris children as disposable items.
His combat style makes full use of Gundam Gusion's heavy armor and thrust, and gave Tekkadan a hard fight with it.
However, he struggled against Mikazuki Augus and his Gundam Barbatos in the battle in the Debris Zone and had to resort to an attempt to take Akihiro Altland's Graze Custom hostage.

A child soldier with the Brewers, a band of space
pirates, and Akihiro Altland's younger brother.

In his younger days, he used to sail the stars with his
family in a merchant ship fleet. Unfortunately, his
parents were killed in a pirate raid, and he was separated
from his big brother Akihiro and sold to the Brewers as
Human Debris where he piloted a Man Rodi.

Subjected to harsh abuse by adults, he clung to the
promise Akihiro had made to come for him one day. As
time marched on and that day never came, he eventually
started to harden his heart.

Later, when the Brewers attacked Tekkadan, he saw his
brother Akihiro again, but attacked out of jealousy for
the surrogate family his brother had gained.

A child soldier with the Brewers, a band of space pirates.

He is a Human Debris boy sold to the Brewers. He was put
through Alaya-Vijnana System surgery, and the Brewers made
him pilot a Man Rodi to assist them with their looting.

He is distinguishable by the scar on his left cheek.

He fought against Tekkadan twice as a member of the Brewers.

A child soldier with the Brewers, a band of space pirates.

He is a Human Debris boy sold to the Brewers. He was put
through Alaya-Vijnana System surgery, and the Brewers made
him pilot a Man Rodi to assist them with their looting.

He believes that humans are reborn after death, and he
told his companions as much when their teammate Pedro was
killed in action.

He fought against Tekkadan twice as a member of the Brewers.

Representative of the Montag Company.
A mysterious figure who hides his face behind a mask. His true identity is McGillis Fareed of Gjallarhorn.
He deemed Tekkadan and Kudelia Aina Bernstein's influence necessary to reform Gjallarhorn, so he provided them with incognito support.
He encountered Kudelia in the Dort Colonies, where he informed her that Fumitan Admoss was a spy sent by Nobliss Gordon. He later made contact with Tekkadan and provided them with a shuttle for Earth reentry in exchange for Martian half metal rights.
Montag is also McGillis' original surname.

Commander-in-chief of the Gjallarhorn Outer Lunar Orbit Regulatory Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
He is the head of House Elion, one of the Seven Stars, and his Arianrhod Fleet has over 40 warships at its disposal.
A broad-minded man who refuses to be bound by outdated customs.
He commands a considerable amount of influence within Gjallarhorn, gaining respect not just from his own men, but also from fellow Seven Stars scion Iok Kujan.
He was quick to take note of McGillis Fareed's background maneuvering within Gjallarhorn.
Once McGillis launched his coup, Rustal employed skillful tactics to bring the Revolutionary Army to its knees.

Commander-in-chief of the Gjallarhorn Outer Lunar Orbit Regulatory Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
He is the head of House Elion, one of the Seven Stars, and his Arianrhod Fleet has over 40 warships at its disposal.
A broad-minded man who refuses to be bound by outdated customs.
He commands a considerable amount of influence within Gjallarhorn, gaining respect not just from his own men, but also from fellow Seven Stars scion Iok Kujan.
He was quick to take note of McGillis Fareed's background maneuvering within Gjallarhorn.
Once McGillis launched his coup, Rustal employed skillful tactics to bring the Revolutionary Army to its knees.

The head of House Kujan, one of the Seven Stars.
He is in charge of commanding the 2nd Fleet, part of the Gjallarhorn Outer Lunar Orbit Regulatory Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
While he has faith in his Fleet Commander, Rustal Elion, the idea of an illegitimate upstart like McGillis Fareed carrying the name of the Seven Stars does not sit well with him.
Headstrong and cocksure, he was responsible for a number of failures, such as contributing to the activation of the Mobile Armor Hashmal on Mars, and being denounced by the Revolutionary Army for bringing the illegal Dainsleif weapon that led to the Turbines' downfall.
However, his men continued to admire him regardless by virtue of both his own character and the popularity of the house leader that preceded him.

The head of House Kujan, one of the Seven Stars.
He is in charge of commanding the 2nd Fleet, part of the Gjallarhorn Outer Lunar Orbit Regulatory Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
While he has faith in his Fleet Commander, Rustal Elion, the idea of an illegitimate upstart like McGillis Fareed carrying the name of the Seven Stars does not sit well with him.
Headstrong and cocksure, he was responsible for a number of failures, such as contributing to the activation of the Mobile Armor Hashmal on Mars, and being denounced by the Revolutionary Army for bringing the illegal Dainsleif weapon that led to the Turbines' downfall.
However, his men continued to admire him regardless by virtue of both his own character and the popularity of the house leader that preceded him.

A new member of Tekkadan.
He joined Tekkadan after the battle of Edmonton, and is distinguished by his pompadour hairstyle.
He joined Tekkadan on a lark and does not take his job too seriously.
He actually has college-level engineering knowledge, unlike most of the rest of the roster, and occasionally shows that he can be a talented technician.
He lost a friend in his first real battle, and started to take issue with the direction Tekkadan was going as it continued to plunge into battle after intense battle.
He eventually made up his mind to leave Tekkadan, but gave up on that when faced with Arianrhod's encirclement and opted to escape with his friends instead.

Leader of the Dawn Horizon Corps, a band of space pirates.
He leads a large pirate fleet with 10 ships and 2,500 personnel between them.
He fears nothing; not even Teiwaz or Gjallarhorn.
He is also a capable pilot who goes out on the frontlines personally.
He gets hired by activist Allium Gyojan to attack Kudelia Aina Bernstein, pitting his group against Tekkadan, her escort.

An executive with Teiwaz, a massive conglomerate.
He is the number-two man in all of Teiwaz, and serves as the representative of JPT Trust, a direct affiliate of Teiwaz.
He did not like the way Naze Turbine and Tekkadan were distinguishing themselves within Teiwaz.
He used Iok Kujan, a man with connections to their predecessor, and got the Turbines falsely accused of transporting illegal weaponry, thus bringing about their downfall.
After Naze's death, he went after Tekkadan, and even plotted to eliminate Teiwaz representative McMurdo Barriston.

A Mobile Suit pilot for the Outer Lunar Orbit Regulatory Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
She is a commoner girl with no family, but her incredible skill at Mobile Suit piloting caught Rustal Elion's attention and landed her a spot in Arianrhod.
She has sworn loyalty to Rustal for taking her in, but her utterly guileless personality leads to Iok Kujan of the Seven Stars relentlessly calling her stupid.
She trained previously under Galan Mossa, and looks up to him and calls him the "Bearded Gentleman."
She pilots the new model Reginlaze and the Reginlaze Julia, and clashed repeatedly against Tekkadan and the reactivated Hashmal.

A Mobile Suit pilot for the Outer Lunar Orbit Regulatory Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
She is a commoner girl with no family, but her incredible skill at Mobile Suit piloting caught Rustal Elion's attention and landed her a spot in Arianrhod.
She has sworn loyalty to Rustal for taking her in, but her utterly guileless personality leads to Iok Kujan of the Seven Stars relentlessly calling her stupid.
She trained previously under Galan Mossa, and looks up to him and calls him the "Bearded Gentleman."
She pilots the new model Reginlaze and the Reginlaze Julia, and clashed repeatedly against Tekkadan and the reactivated Hashmal.

A new member of Tekkadan.
He joined Tekkadan after the battle of Edmonton, originally coming from the Mars slums.
He had a brother figure named Builth who committed suicide after a failed Alaya-Vijnana System surgery.
He works to give his fellow slumdwellers hope in Builth's place.
He originally saw Mikazuki Augus as a rival, but changed his mind after seeing the overwhelming difference in skill between them in combat.
After that, he began accompanying Mikazuki around and finding ways to accommodate his physical handicaps.
He racked up a considerable amount of real combat experience in a Shiden and a Hekija.
When Arianrhod launched a search-and-destroy operation, he was right there with Mikazuki to engage them.

McGillis Fareed's second-in-command, appointed Commander of the Outer Earth Orbit Regulatory Joint Fleet.
He has known McGillis since his auditing division days.
He acts as McGillis' right-hand man, and is a calm man of few words.
He supports McGillis' aim of reforming Gjallarhorn because of all the discrimination he has endured as a colonial.
He is a highly skilled Mobile Suit pilot capable of mastering challenging machines like the Schwalbe Graze and Helmwige Reincar.
Given his familiarity with affairs in the Outer Sphere, he was dispatched to the Mars Sphere and fought alongside Tekkadan to put down the Dawn Horizon Corps.

A Mobile Suit pilot for the Outer Lunar Orbit Regulatory Joint Fleet Arianrhod.
A man of mystery who conceals his face behind a mask, no one knows his personal history.
He demonstrates extraordinary piloting skill in battle using a suit named after him, Gundam Vidar.
He seems to be connected to McGillis Fareed in some way- he repeatedly passes on tips to his commander Rustal Elion regarding McGillis' activities.
Fellow pilot Julieta Juris viewed him with suspicion at first due to his unsociableness and background, but they gradually grew closer as the two fought together.

A young mercenary who wears a mechanical prosthetic in place of his lost right arm.
Five years ago, Argi lost his family as well as his right arm during an attack by a Gundam Frame, and he became a mercenary to seek revenge.
He visited Ted Morugaton after being asked to assassinate the man, but he ends up getting attacked by the Triaina, piloted by another assassin.
Under Ted's orders, he piloted the Gundam Astaroth entrusted to him by Volco Warren and engages the enemy assassin.
To repay Ted for sacrificing his life to protect him, Argi officially becomes the pilot of the Astaroth to gain the strength he needs to exact vengeance against the Gundam.
Argi has a strong sense of responsibility, but is alsoblunt and terrible with women.
Although he has not undergone the Alaya-Vijnana System surgery, he can connect the nerves of his prosthetic to his Mobile Suit to take advantage of higher reaction speed while piloting.

A young mercenary who wears a mechanical prosthetic in place of his lost right arm.
Five years ago, Argi lost his family as well as his right arm during an attack by a Gundam Frame, and he became a mercenary to seek revenge.
He visited Ted Morugaton after being asked to assassinate the man, but he ends up getting attacked by the Triaina, piloted by another assassin.
Under Ted's orders, he piloted the Gundam Astaroth entrusted to him by Volco Warren and engages the enemy assassin.
To repay Ted for sacrificing his life to protect him, Argi officially becomes the pilot of the Astaroth to gain the strength he needs to exact vengeance against the Gundam.
Argi has a strong sense of responsibility, but is alsoblunt and terrible with women.
Although he has not undergone the Alaya-Vijnana System surgery, he can connect the nerves of his prosthetic to his Mobile Suit to take advantage of higher reaction speed while piloting.

A Human Debris who works as a mercenary with his younger sister.
Composed and serious, Sampo dreams of casting off the stigma of being Human Debris and sending his sister to school.
In Mobile Suit combat, he pilots a Hakuri Rodi and works in sync with Yuhana to support her aggressive style.
After attacking Liarina Morugaton under the orders of Rosario Leone, he battled the Gundam Astaroth piloted by Argi Mirage.
He later helps Argi, who was being targeted by Rosario, under the orders of Gianmarco Salerno who had his sister was held hostage.

A Human Debris who works as a mercenary with her older brother.
Hedonistic and dynamic, she values her older brother Sampo immensely in spite of his completely opposite personality.
In Mobile Suit combat, she pilots a Hakuri Rodi and works in sync with her brother, always eager to take point.
After attacking Liarina Morugaton under the orders of Rosario Leone, she battled the Gundam Astaroth piloted by Argi Mirage.
She is later captured by Gianmarco Salerno, who uses her as a hostage to force Sampo to work for him.

An executive of the large corporation Tanto Tempo, based in the Avalanche Colony Cluster on the Moon.
Gianmarco is one of the six executives on Tanto Tempo's Board of Directors, and he is in charge of the Transportation Department.
He is known to prefer a brute-force approach, and even pilots a Mobile Suit to fight pirates when he is in the Outer Spheres.
Because he expressed objections to Ted Morugaton's approach, which involved building a good relationship with Gjallarhorn by using a lot of money, he was suspected of being the mastermind behind Ted's assassination.

An executive of the large corporation Tanto Tempo, based in the Avalanche colonies on the Moon.
Rosario is one of the six executives on Tanto Tempo's Board of Directors, and he is in charge of the Banking Department (Western Bank).
Although he was once a Mobile Suit pilot for a crew of space pirates, he was discovered by Ted Morugaton and ended up in his employ.
After that, Rosario quickly distinguished himself and soon became Ted's right-hand man.
However, that was not enough to satisfy him, and he secretly longed to take Ted's place entirely.
Rosario's skills as a pilot are as sharp as ever, and he pilots the Gundam Vual in combat.

A maid who works for Buburio Inshina.
Although talented, she is also mysterious. 
For example, she remains calm even after being attacked by enemies, and is highly skilled at piloting spaceships.
Under Buburio's orders, she accompanies Liarina Morugaton's group to Avalanche 1.

A young mercenary who pilots the Gundam Dantalion.
Zaza attacks Argi Mirage's group and demands that they hand over the Cerulean sisters, who are under their protection.
However, his intentions are not clear.
Although he fell back once in the face of support from Gianmarco Salerno, he later hired the Hakuri siblings and once again tried to capture the sisters.
His Mobile Suit piloting skills are undeniable, and his manner of speaking suggests a privileged upbringing.

The heir of the Zalmfort family, the second-most influential family after the Seven Stars.
Zadiel is the son of Sylt Zalmfort and possesses a strong sense of justice and sincere personality.
As a result of an accident involving a shuttle his younger sister Mina was on, he became suspicious of his father and took off in the Gundam Dantalion.
He later calls himself the mercenary Zaza Fossil, independently tracks down his missing sister, and learns the truth behind the shuttle accident from her.

The commander of Ollerus, Gjallarhorn's internal control team, which is independently controlled by the Nadira family.
A sincere and calm soldier, he pilots the Oltlinde, which has been passed down by the Jizin family.
He pursued Mina Zalmfort in an effort to clear the Nadira family's name.
After intentionally surrendering to Tanto Tempo to confirm that she is still alive, he contacts Ville Klaassen to try to get to the bottom of the crimes.

The next head of the Nadira family, an illustrious member of Gjallarhorn.
She has actually already been entrusted with control of the family by the previous generation and is therefore its head for all intents and purposes.
In addition, she is engaged to Mina Zalmfort in a political arrangement.
Deira is also a talented Mobile Suit pilot and pilots the Gundam Gremory, which is owned by the Nadira family.
Her father is falsely accused of collusion with the African Union and detained by the Supervisory Bureau.
She is also stripped of her power within Gjallarhorn and ends up in hiding out on an island near the Antarctic.

A youth born and raised in Radonitsa, a floating colony at the planet Venus.
Having lost both parents, Wistario was forced to inherit leadership of the colony management company Afam Facilities at an early age.
Cheerful and decisive, Wistario dreams of one day turning Venus into a tourist destination.

A four-year-old girl who lives with her family at Fólkvangr. She is the daughter of Elnora and Nadim. Ericht is an innocent child, and so curious that her parents sometimes don't know what to do with her.
The Gundam Lfrith's development manager, who has been temporarily transferred to the Vanadis Institute from the mobile suit development company Ochs Earth. He is the father of the Samaya family.
A Vanadis Institute researcher. She is Elnora's junior as a test pilot, and a close friend of Nyla.

A pilot in the Mobile Suit Development Council's Dominicus special forces team. 
He demonstrates his ability using the mobile suit Beguir-Beu, produced by the Grassley company.

A second-year student in the piloting department, who transferred to the school from Mercury. 
Suletta is timid and somewhat lacks communication skills.
She is the pilot of the Gundam Aerial developed on Mercury.

A second-year student in the piloting department, who transferred to the school from Mercury. Suletta is timid and somewhat lacks communication skills.
She is the pilot of the Gundam Aerial developed on Mercury.

An attractive and academically distinguished second-year student in the management strategy department. Miorine is the only daughter of Delling Rembran, the president of the Benerit Group and chairman of the school's board.
She has a strong rebellious feeling towards her father.

An heir to Jeturk Heavy Machinery, one of the group's three branches, and a third-year student in the piloting department.
Guel has a rough temperament and is quick to anger. 
As the ace pilot of Jeturk House, he has absolute confidence in his own skills.

An heir to Jeturk Heavy Machinery, one of the group's three branches, and a third-year student in the piloting department.
Guel has a rough temperament and is quick to anger. 
As the ace pilot of Jeturk House, he has absolute confidence in his own skills.

An heir to Jeturk Heavy Machinery, one of the group's three branches, and a third-year student in the piloting department.
Guel has a rough temperament and is quick to anger. 
As the ace pilot of Jeturk House, he has absolute confidence in his own skills.

The top pilot backed by Peil Technologies, one of the group's three branches. A third-year student in the piloting department. Elan is a taciturn and solitary person, who doesn't open his heart to anyone in school. He has an interest in Suletta.

An adopted child of the CEO of Grassley Defense Systems, one of the group's three branches. A third-year student in the piloting department who leads Grassley House, Shaddiq is a smooth-talking ladies' man with a carefree manner. Although still a student, Shaddiq has shown his skill in business too, and he is a candidate for next generation executive.

A first-year student in the piloting department, who belongs to Earth House. 
She is nicknamed "Chuchu." She hates the Spacians who discriminate against Earthians, and often gets into fights. 
Chuchu pilots a unique restored older-generation Demi Trainer.

A third-year student in the management strategy department, and the leader of Earth House. Martin is an indecisive and somewhat unreliable leader, who has difficulty handling the strong personalities of his House members.
A second-year student in the mechanical department, who belongs to Earth House. Ojelo is cheerful and encouraging. He is also a gambler who sinks his living allowance into betting on duels.
A third-year student in the mechanical department, who belongs to Earth House. Till is calm and taciturn, an unsung hero who supports the other members of his House.

A first-year student in the management strategy department, who belongs to Earth House. Lilique has a cheerful and easygoing personality. She loves to gossip about school romances, and is popular with the male students.
A third-year student in the piloting department, who belongs to Grassley House. She is a talented young woman who serves as Shaddiq's right hand, assisting him in his work both in and out of school.

Her Gunpla is the Beaggai.
She thinks of herself as Haru's girlfriend, but it is unclear whether he would agree.

A mysterious boy who Sei Iori met in the street.
He is pushy, selfish, hot-tempered, and does not possess an abundance of common sense.
When he stumbled into participating in a Gunpla Battle, he demonstrated superior piloting skills despite it being his first one.

One of the Seven Mushas , an elite team within the Gundam Forces.
Shogundam called upon the Seven Mushas when the Darkness Corps, an evil feudal army led by Dark Shogun, invaded.
The first to receive the title musha, he is the strongest musha of the seven, surpassing the others in every martial art.

The Knight Gundam armed with three legendary relics: the Armor of Mist, Flame Sword, and Shield of Strength.
When three stars came into alignment, the power of the relics increased tenfold, and the Knight Gundam became more powerful than even the Black Dragon.
Unfortunately, this power exacted a heavy toll on his body.

The monstrous form of the Satan Gundam also known as "Fear Itself."
It possesses such overwhelming power that the Knight Gundam has no hope of defeating it without the three relics.

A Lacroan warrior in the service of King Revil that accompanied the Knight Gundam on his quest to vanquish Satan Gundam.
A powerful fighter confident in his own strength, he possesses a spirit made of steel.

A knowledgeable Lacroan priest.
He quested with the Knight Gundam along with the Warrior Guncannon.
He is not only skilled in offensive magic but recovery magic as well, making him an invaluable party member.

A fairy that had been fighting the Zeon family in a Lacroan forest.
When he learned that the Knight Gundam and his party were on a quest to vanquish Satan Gundam, he joined them in order to lend his strength.
He is an expert archer, and his crossbow played a key role in the battle with a legendary giant.

A knight of the Zeon family in the service of Satan Gundam who stood in the way the Knight Gundam and his party on their way to Zeon Black Castle.
He used a glowing red sword as a weapon to attack members of the Union.
His right arm conceals an electromagnetic whip called a heat rod.

A knight of the Zeon family in the service of Satan Gundam.
Prideful and ruthless, there is no low he will not stoop to in order to win.
He usually wields a rapier in battle, but when backed into a corner, he will also attack with missiles installed in his shield.

A warrior of the Zeon family in the service of Satan Gundam who is equipped with an axe and shield.
Having witnessed Satan Gundam fuse with a dragon, he knows the secret of the Black Dragon.

A knight of the Zeon family in the service of Satan Gundam.
His abilities are second only to his retainer, Sazabi, and he stood in the way of the Knight Gundam and his party when they infiltrated Zeon Black Castle.
He is also strong enough to effortlessly swing an oversized sword.

A warrior of the Zeon family who effortlessly swings a long axe.
A powerful fighter confident in his own strength, he uses brute force to bring his axe down in a special move called the Power Slash.
He ambushed the Knight Gundam and his party on their way to Zeon Black Castle.

A knight of the Zeon family who stood in the way of the Knight Gundam and his party in Zeon Black Castle.
He specializes in magical sword attacks that combine magic with swordsmanship and proved a worthy foe for the Knight Gundam's party.

A red knight who serves as a retainer to Satan Gundam.
Excelling in both swordsmanship and magic, he is the most powerful knight under the Demon King, and he stood in the way of the Knight Gundam and his party when they arrived to vanquish Satan Gundam.

The feared Demon King who is said to have descended upon the Saddrac World in a flash of lightning.
Rarely making an appearance in person, he controls the monsters and knights that comprise the Zeon family from Zeon Black Castle, where he spreads his evil influence throughout the peaceful Lacroa Kingdom.
He attacks by concentrating magical energy into his Demon King Staff, which enables him to launch attacks from a distance.
He is actually the evil half of the Musha Gundam Mk-III. Split in half after his arrival from another world, he is an opposing force to the Knight Gundam.
The true nature of his identity has yet to be revealed, however...

The good half of the Musha Gundam Mk-III, he was split in two by Sieg Zeon.
Although he lost all memory apart from the name Gundam, he decided to follow his sense of justice and fight the Zeon family.
Since he was also able to pull out the Electrospear said to have been wielded by the hero, he set out against the Demon King as the Lacroan Hero.

The good half of the Musha Gundam Mk-III, he was split in two by Sieg Zeon.

A mysterious Gundam that suddenly appeared before a shiftless Gundam team on the Magellan continent following their defeat at the hands of the Zatarion regular army's Bloody Zaku.
He proceeded to whip the Gundam team into shape, forming an elite team called G-ARMS.

He is a member of "Dragon's Watch," a vigilante group in the Shu Area, which is not targeted by Etiolation Trinity MS too often.
After meeting Guan Yu Nu Gundam and Zhang Fei God Gundam, he makes an important decision: To save the world.
He eventually starts to gain comrades who are drawn to his strong passion.

Produced by the Federation Forces' Murasame Newtype Laboratory as the first ever Cyber-Newtype, this boy is also known by the name "Proto Zero."
He has supreme confidence in his abilities, and piloting skills to match. 
However, the surgeries needed to make him an artificial Newtype left his psyche and physique very fragile.
Searching desperately for a cure, he defected to Zeon, taking the Cyber-Newtype data and technology along with him. Even so, he feels responsible for the effect of his actions on fellow Cyber-Newtype Leila Raymond.

An ace pilot who can adapt to any situation with his calm and collected manner. 
His strong leadership skills have helped him lead his comrades to victory on many occasions, truly earning his "Ace" title.

A proud mercenary, he is confident in his piloting abilities. 
He takes pride in his work and has the skills to match. He has unwavering confidence in his abilities and is always on the lookout for someone stronger than himself.

A pilot who fights with a keen sense and a strong will.
She is serious, kind, and has no love of fighting, but that does not mean she lacks the willpower and determination to pull through long and difficult battles.
To end the war, she overcame her hesitancy and plunged into the fight.
Perhaps someday, Lady Luck will be on her side...

Known to be a lively and active character, she is capable of easily breaking the ice with anyone she meets.
She is also a great impressionist, and often uses her skill during battle to ease tensions. Although she lacks combat experience and may not be geared for it, she may prove useful for boosting morale during long battles.
She is adept at impersonating people from any Generation, although it is a mystery how she gained this ability.

While he detests the act of war itself, this youth with a strong sense of justice chooses to fight to put an end to this senseless bloodshed.
With a gifted sense of hand-to-hand combat, it is said there is no one who can match his skills in close-quarters combat.
He can supposedly hear the "voice of the universe," and has gone missing after following this "voice" in the past.

A seasoned veteran with a great deal of combat experience.
His experience allows him to provide skillful support to younger members and he has garnered their trust.
Though he may be cynical, he has the ability to make quick decisions and complete missions effectively.

A highly-skilled mechanic with copious amounts of knowledge and experience with machines.
She has the disposition of a big sister and has saved many on the battlefield with her expertise. 
The others have come to admire her.

Despite his frail appearance, he is an incredibly active and strong-hearted young man who does not easily give in to adversity.
Although his combat skills may be lacking at the moment, his high adaptability should trigger his hidden strengths over time.
These may be just the powers he needs to succeed in helping those he cares about.

Foul-mouthed and appearing uninterested in battle, this skilled pilot may seem a little strange at first.
Through his demeanor, he is able to lure his enemies closer to him, saving his comrades from danger by drawing attention away from them. However, it is unknown whether or not he does this intentionally.

Calm and cool when it comes to battle decisions, she has all the qualities of a good aide. Although she has the skills, she lacks experience in actual combat, and her cautious nature can sometimes hinder her in the face of unexpected events. 
With more experience, she definitely has the potential to bloom.

Initially contracted as an operator, she somehow ended up piloting a Mobile Suit and was sent out to battle. This unlucky woman cried day and night as she fought in Mobile Suit battles until it eventually became her way of relieving stress.
As an operator, she no longer has any opportunities to work as a pilot. Losing this outlet for stress relief has made her more restless lately.

She tries to act as a lady of nobility, but actually has a violent temper. She uses overly-polite language and an overbearing personality to get what she wants out of life.
Stay on her good side and she is a great ally, but get on her bad side and her true persona will be unleashed like a wild beast, out of control.
Recently, she has had opportunities to meet genuine ladies of nobility. 
However, she has become even less tolerant of those who might call her a phony, attacking them without exception.

A proud knight who remains honorable, especially during battle. She is an intelligent person who was trained in the art of sword-fighting by her beloved brother.
Her talent now rivals even his, but he has fallen ill and she is now the head of the Shultz family. 
She commits herself to her duties as a knight, determined not to tarnish the family name.

Because he focused more on getting laughs than training as a pilot or commander, he only has the bare minimum of skills. He is also a coward and quick to flee, therefore not suitable for battle.
However, his amazing animal instincts have guided him through life. 
Perhaps it can be argued that he is of a breed superior to human beings...

A kind-hearted woman with a mysterious aura, she gives off a sense of abnormal power and is a source of comfort on the battlefield. 
She is always aware of her surroundings and gives support to comrades where necessary.
Her keen sniper skills have also saved the lives of many teammates. She often takes on the role of a guardian to others.

A battle-worn hero with many glittering achievements under his belt, he is also a hard worker who obeys the rules.
He uses his experience to teach and support, while also standing on the front line of battle.
He is proud of his home country, and is prepared to die for it.
His middle initial, "M," stands for Mitchell.

Having been born into a noble family, her personality can be oppressive, and she frequently gives others the wrong impression. However, this is something she is used to, and she keenly feels the divide between herself and others.
Due to this, she wants to become closer to her comrades and tries hard to behave like a "normal" girl.

A proud and dangerous man who considers anyone other than himself to be a lesser being. 
With excellent fighting skills and a plethora of experiences and achievements obtained through fierce battles, he is an integral part of the team, despite his trouble getting along with others.
He would never shoot a comrade, so perhaps he does have some warped sense of morality.

A highly valuable battle expert and fleet commander who has an effeminate manner of speech.
He seems to favor those of the same sex and never lets his targets escape, whether or not they are enemies.
This has earned him the nickname "Hunter."

An innocent and pure girl who is too young to have any fighting skills, but is friendly to everyone and enjoys calling them by nicknames.
She is especially fond of Sys Mitville, and goes around calling her "Mimi," causing Sys some embarrassment.
Though it started out as a one-sided friendship, they have been chatting about their hobbies and interests recently. 
They are friends now, but Reese hopes they can be even more open with each other.

A reserved girl who detests fighting but has no other choice. 
Though she hates herself for it, she reluctantly engages in battle.
She is a rather poor communicator and therefore values the undivided attention and friendship of Kachua Reese, although she lacks the words to express this sentiment.

Having fought in numerous battles from a young age, he exhibits a calm and hard-boiled personality and is now philosophical about war.
However, he is fond of his comrades and is always there to support them as a big brother figure.

With her dislike for fighting, her demeanor may come off as placid. However, she is an experienced pilot with many battles under her belt.
She is a strong-willed woman who takes on a sisterly role within the team.

Cheerful and full of energy, she is even capable of finding enjoyment during battles. 
Although this helps lighten up those around her, she sometimes offends people without meaning to. 
At heart, she is a friendly girl who wants nothing more than world peace.

A calm and quiet boy who is not easily fazed, he is also a genius who can calculate almost anything. 
His personality often comes across as cold to others, but deep down he is always calculating the best way to protect his comrades in battle.

A veteran officer who always keeps a cool head while in action. 
He has exceptional fighting skills and is able to instruct comrades gently but firmly. 
He is a valuable asset, but prefers to stay in the background, serving as a loyal and reliable aide to the commander.

A communications specialist with expert operating skills, this man is trusted by his comrades for his ability to accurately assess a situation on the ground.
However, his bubbly and happy personality makes him quite the chatterbox during battle, and recently people have been cutting his communications short.
Not realizing this, he gets upset every time he thinks people are ignoring him.

Loved by everyone on the team, this bright, forward- thinking hard worker enjoys an almost mascot-like status. 
However, she wants to be taken more seriously and tries her best to be useful, even if she's not very skilled.
Her presence on the front line makes people nervous, and she ends up being overprotected by her comrades.
She is also known to talk like a boy from time to time.

A hardcore mechanic, his reason for being on the battlefield is so that he can tweak and modify the latest and rarest mecha to his heart's content.
Although his skills are substantial, he is quite lazy at heart, and has no interest in anything other than mechanics. 
He has a tendency to prioritize machines over people, getting excited only when he sees interesting mecha. 
He is often looking for an opportunity to make off with rare components even in the midst of battle.

A gigantic weapon of destruction shrouded in complete mystery, just like the Haro.
